metadata.name: WIDGETS
metadata.comment: Cleaned and curated widget data on Near Social.

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

SIGNER_ID.name: SIGNER_ID
SIGNER_ID.comment: The signer is the account that originally signed the transaction that began the blockchain activity, which may or may not include cross-contract calls. If a function calls results in several cross-contract calls, think of the signer as the account that pushed over the first domino in that chain reaction.

WIDGET_NAME.name: WIDGET_NAME
WIDGET_NAME.comment: The name of the widget as saved on Near Social. There is no unique identifier for widgets, so if the name is changed by the developer the next transaction record will reflect the new name.

SOURCE_CODE.name: SOURCE_CODE
SOURCE_CODE.comment: The source code for the widget, in JavaScript.

METADATA.name: METADATA
METADATA.comment: Metadata (object) for this transaction. May be null, especially for Near Social Widgets.

BRANCH.name: BRANCH
BRANCH.comment: The branch, if any, of the widget module. May be null.

WIDGET_MODULES_USED.name: WIDGET_MODULES_USED
WIDGET_MODULES_USED.comment: References any other widgets used in the targeted widget/Near Social app. May be null.

WIDGET_URL.name: WIDGET_URL
WIDGET_URL.comment: A direct link to the widget. Note, if the name was later changed, this URL will be an invalid link.

_SOURCE_DATA.name: _SOURCE_DATA
_SOURCE_DATA.comment: A silver-level column with the fulll (unparsed) Widget object.

_NODE_DATA.name: _NODE_DATA
_NODE_DATA.comment: The data object that the top level node contains.

_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.name: _PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER
_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Block number grouping for ingestion partition

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp for when the block was inserted into the data warehouse by the Chainwalker.

SOCIAL_WIDGETS_ID.name: SOCIAL_WIDGETS_ID
SOCIAL_WIDGETS_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

_INVOCATION_ID.name: _INVOCATION_ID
_INVOCATION_ID.comment: A job ID to identify the run that last modified a record.

metadata.name: EZ_SUPPLY
metadata.comment:
Deprecating Soon - these tables previously supported NEAR Atlas which is no longer live. These tables will be removed on Febreary 1, 2025.
This EZ_SUPPLY table will be retained and migrated to CORE.

UTC_DATE.name: UTC_DATE
UTC_DATE.comment: Datestamp, assuming UTC-0, of the record.

TOTAL_SUPPLY.name: TOTAL_SUPPLY
TOTAL_SUPPLY.comment: The total_supply taken from block headers.

TOTAL_NONSTAKED_SUPPLY.name: TOTAL_NONSTAKED_SUPPLY
TOTAL_NONSTAKED_SUPPLY.comment: Total supply less total staked supply of NEAR.

TOTAL_LOCKED_SUPPLY.name: TOTAL_LOCKED_SUPPLY
TOTAL_LOCKED_SUPPLY.comment: Total NEAR locked by lockup.near disbursements.

STAKED_LOCKED_SUPPLY.name: STAKED_LOCKED_SUPPLY
STAKED_LOCKED_SUPPLY.comment: NEAR supply that is locked via a lockup and staked to a node.

NON_STAKED_LOCKED_SUPPLY.name: NON_STAKED_LOCKED_SUPPLY
NON_STAKED_LOCKED_SUPPLY.comment: NEAR supply that is locked via a lockup but not staked.

STAKED_CIRCULATING_SUPPLY.name: STAKED_CIRCULATING_SUPPLY
STAKED_CIRCULATING_SUPPLY.comment: Non-locked (from a lockup.near disbursement) and staked to a node.

NONSTAKED_CIRCULATING_SUPPLY.name: NONSTAKED_CIRCULATING_SUPPLY
NONSTAKED_CIRCULATING_SUPPLY.comment: NEAR supply that is neither locked nor staked.

PERC_LOCKED_SUPPLY.name: PERC_LOCKED_SUPPLY
PERC_LOCKED_SUPPLY.comment:
Percentage of supply locked.  
total_locked_supply / total_supply

PERC_CIRCULATING_SUPPLY.name: PERC_CIRCULATING_SUPPLY
PERC_CIRCULATING_SUPPLY.comment:
Percentage of NEAR circulating.
circulating_supply / total_supply

PERC_STAKED_LOCKED.name: PERC_STAKED_LOCKED
PERC_STAKED_LOCKED.comment:
Percentage of supply both staked and locked, out of all locked supply.  
locked_and_staked_supply / total_locked_supply

PERC_STAKED_CIRCULATING.name: PERC_STAKED_CIRCULATING
PERC_STAKED_CIRCULATING.comment:
Percentage of supply staked and not locked, of circulating supply.
nonlocked_and_staked_supply / circulating_supply

metadata.name: FACT_PRICES_OHLC_HOURLY
metadata.comment: A comprehensive fact table holding id and provider specific open, high, low, close hourly prices, from multiple providers. This data set includes raw, non-transformed data coming directly from the provider APIs and rows are not intended to be unique. As a result, there may be data quality issues persisting in the APIs that flow through to this fact based model. If you are interested in using a curated data set instead, please utilize ez_prices_hourly.

ASSET_ID.name: ASSET_ID
ASSET_ID.comment: The unique identifier representing the asset.

HOUR.name: HOUR
HOUR.comment: Hour that the price was recorded at.

OPEN.name: OPEN
OPEN.comment: Opening price of the recorded hour in USD.

HIGH.name: HIGH
HIGH.comment: Highest price of the recorded hour in USD

LOW.name: LOW
LOW.comment: Lowest price of the recorded hour in USD

CLOSE.name: CLOSE
CLOSE.comment: Closing price of the recorded hour in USD

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

FACT_PRICES_OHLC_HOURLY_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

metadata.name: TOKEN_TRANSFER_DEPOSIT
metadata.comment: This table records all the positive Deposit events from FunctionCalls.

ACTION_ID.name: ACTION_ID
ACTION_ID.comment: The `action_id` is a concatenation of `receipt_id `and the index of the action within a receipt. This is unique for each record. The primary hash depends on the source of the action, as both a transaction and a receipt can process actions.

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

PREDECESSOR_ID.name: PREDECESSOR_ID
PREDECESSOR_ID.comment:
This is the account that was the immediate caller to the smart contract. If this is a simple transaction (no cross-contract calls) from alice.near to banana.near, the smart contract at banana.near considers Alice the predecessor. In this case, Alice would also be the signer.  
  
Source: https://docs.near.org/tutorials/crosswords/beginner/actions#predecessor-signer-and-current-account

SIGNER_ID.name: SIGNER_ID
SIGNER_ID.comment: The signer is the account that originally signed the transaction that began the blockchain activity, which may or may not include cross-contract calls. If a function calls results in several cross-contract calls, think of the signer as the account that pushed over the first domino in that chain reaction.

RECEIVER_ID.name: RECEIVER_ID
RECEIVER_ID.comment:
The address of the NEAR account that the receipt was executed on.
Note, this differs from `tx_receiver` and is associated with the receipt within a transaction. It is often a contract on which a method is executed by the `signer_id`.

AMOUNT_UNADJ.name: AMOUNT_UNADJ
AMOUNT_UNADJ.comment: An unadjusted amount (of tokens, price, etc.) for the relevant record. This is the number as it appears on chain and is not decimal adjusted.

AMOUNT_ADJ.name: AMOUNT_ADJ
AMOUNT_ADJ.comment: A decimal adjusted amount (of tokens, price, etc.).

RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED.name: RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED
RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED.comment: A boolean indicating whether the receipt was successfully processed, based on the presence of a Failure message.

_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.name: _PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER
_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Block number grouping for ingestion partition

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp for when the block was inserted into the data warehouse by the Chainwalker.

TOKEN_TRANSFER_DEPOSIT_ID.name: TOKEN_TRANSFER_DEPOSIT_ID
TOKEN_TRANSFER_DEPOSIT_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

_INVOCATION_ID.name: _INVOCATION_ID
_INVOCATION_ID.comment: A job ID to identify the run that last modified a record.

metadata.name: FACT_LOCKUP_ACTIONS
metadata.comment: This table records all disbursements by the contract lockup.near.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

DEPOSIT.name: DEPOSIT
DEPOSIT.comment:
Sum of all NEAR tokens transferred from the signing account to the receiver account during receipt processing. This includes tokens sent in a Transfer action(s), and tokens sent in a FunctionCall action(s) with a deposit attached.

In raw number format with 24 decimal places, to adjust divide by POW(10,24) or multiply by 1e-24.

LOCKUP_ACCOUNT_ID.name: LOCKUP_ACCOUNT_ID
LOCKUP_ACCOUNT_ID.comment: A vault created by the lockup action that holds the distributed NEAR tokens until the end of the lockup duration.

OWNER_ACCOUNT_ID.name: OWNER_ACCOUNT_ID
OWNER_ACCOUNT_ID.comment: The wallet address of the owner for the locked tokens, per the initial distribution.

LOCKUP_DURATION.name: LOCKUP_DURATION
LOCKUP_DURATION.comment: The duration of the lockup period.

LOCKUP_TIMESTAMP.name: LOCKUP_TIMESTAMP
LOCKUP_TIMESTAMP.comment: The initial lockup timestamp, in epoch time.

LOCKUP_TIMESTAMP_NTZ.name: LOCKUP_TIMESTAMP_NTZ
LOCKUP_TIMESTAMP_NTZ.comment: Timestamp of the start of the lockup period, converted to UTC time.

RELEASE_DURATION.name: RELEASE_DURATION
RELEASE_DURATION.comment: The duration until the locked tokens can be released.

VESTING_SCHEDULE.name: VESTING_SCHEDULE
VESTING_SCHEDULE.comment: Legacy/no longer used. The vesting schedule for the locked tokens.

TRANSFERS_INFORMATION.name: TRANSFERS_INFORMATION
TRANSFERS_INFORMATION.comment: A dictionary of information about the parameters allowing or limiting transfers of the locked NEAR.

FACT_LOCKUP_ACTIONS_ID.name: FACT_LOCKUP_ACTIONS_ID
FACT_LOCKUP_ACTIONS_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: EZ_BRIDGE_ACTIVITY
metadata.comment: Modeled bridge activity, tracking tokens through the NEAR Ecosystem. Token labels and USD Price appended to record, where available.

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

TOKEN_ADDRESS.name: TOKEN_ADDRESS
TOKEN_ADDRESS.comment: The contract address corresponding to the token, view details of the contract by following this address on the chain explorer.

AMOUNT_UNADJ.name: AMOUNT_UNADJ
AMOUNT_UNADJ.comment: An unadjusted amount (of tokens, price, etc.) for the relevant record. This is the number as it appears on chain and is not decimal adjusted.

AMOUNT_ADJ.name: AMOUNT_ADJ
AMOUNT_ADJ.comment: A decimal adjusted amount (of tokens, price, etc.).

SYMBOL.name: SYMBOL
SYMBOL.comment: The shorthand symbol for the token, as used by common price feeds and exchanges.

AMOUNT.name: AMOUNT
AMOUNT.comment:
The amount of tokens transferred
If in the ez_ view, this number is decimal adjusted to actual amount. If in silver level or a fact_ view, the raw amount is presented

AMOUNT_USD.name: AMOUNT_USD
AMOUNT_USD.comment: Token amount for the record decimal adjusted and multiplied by a price in USD, if available.

DESTINATION_ADDRESS.name: DESTINATION_ADDRESS
DESTINATION_ADDRESS.comment: The address tokens are being sent to, in a transfer or bridge transaction.

SOURCE_ADDRESS.name: SOURCE_ADDRESS
SOURCE_ADDRESS.comment:
The originating address from which tokens are being sent, in a transfer or bridge transaction.  

This addressed is ascertained as best as possible, but may be null depending on the available information.  

Example 1 - tx CzPRdoiU74bo9yqqt9fCP5Xdab2mPVqxr9gU5xeaQA4a stores just "signer_public_key 0x0x1589…f694" on chain.  

Example 2 - tx FA7tQeGQnhminbas28YgaizwmT4vnEUiCr3vdXmN3Xjn does not log any signing address at all.

PLATFORM.name: PLATFORM
PLATFORM.comment: The protocol utilized for the operation, likely a swap or bridge.

BRIDGE_ADDRESS.name: BRIDGE_ADDRESS
BRIDGE_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the deployed contract for the token, nft, or other smart contract.

DESTINATION_CHAIN.name: DESTINATION_CHAIN
DESTINATION_CHAIN.comment: The recipient blockchain in a bridge transaction.

SOURCE_CHAIN.name: SOURCE_CHAIN
SOURCE_CHAIN.comment: The originating blockchain in a bridge transaction.

METHOD_NAME.name: METHOD_NAME
METHOD_NAME.comment: Name of the method(s) approved for use. NULL if permission is FullAccess.

DIRECTION.name: DIRECTION
DIRECTION.comment:
Direction of funds through a bridge. Inbound = tokens bridged to Near ecosystem.

Note - for the Rainbow bridge, inbound means Near or Aurora as tokens pass through Near to get to Aurora.

RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED.name: RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED
RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED.comment: A boolean indicating whether the receipt was successfully processed, based on the presence of a Failure message.

EZ_BRIDGE_ACTIVITY_ID.name: EZ_BRIDGE_ACTIVITY_ID
EZ_BRIDGE_ACTIVITY_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

metadata.name: FACT_WIDGET_DEPLOYMENTS
metadata.comment: Cleaned and curated widget deployments on Near Social.

ACTION_ID_SOCIAL.name: ACTION_ID_SOCIAL
ACTION_ID_SOCIAL.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

SIGNER_ID.name: SIGNER_ID
SIGNER_ID.comment: The signer is the account that originally signed the transaction that began the blockchain activity, which may or may not include cross-contract calls. If a function calls results in several cross-contract calls, think of the signer as the account that pushed over the first domino in that chain reaction.

WIDGET_NAME.name: WIDGET_NAME
WIDGET_NAME.comment: The name of the widget as saved on Near Social. There is no unique identifier for widgets, so if the name is changed by the developer the next transaction record will reflect the new name.

SOURCE_CODE.name: SOURCE_CODE
SOURCE_CODE.comment: The source code for the widget, in JavaScript.

METADATA.name: METADATA
METADATA.comment: Metadata (object) for this transaction. May be null, especially for Near Social Widgets.

BRANCH.name: BRANCH
BRANCH.comment: The branch, if any, of the widget module. May be null.

WIDGET_MODULES_USED.name: WIDGET_MODULES_USED
WIDGET_MODULES_USED.comment: References any other widgets used in the targeted widget/Near Social app. May be null.

WIDGET_URL.name: WIDGET_URL
WIDGET_URL.comment: A direct link to the widget. Note, if the name was later changed, this URL will be an invalid link.

FACT_WIDGET_DEPLOYMENTS_ID.name: FACT_WIDGET_DEPLOYMENTS_ID
FACT_WIDGET_DEPLOYMENTS_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

metadata.name: DIM_ASSET_METADATA
metadata.comment: A comprehensive dimensional table holding asset metadata and other relevant details pertaining to each id, from multiple providers. This data set includes raw, non-transformed data coming directly from the provider APIs and rows are not intended to be unique. As a result, there may be data quality issues persisting in the APIs that flow through to this dimensional model. If you are interested in using a curated data set instead, please utilize ez_asset_metadata.

DIM_ASSET_METADATA_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

metadata.name: ATLAS_SUPPLY_LOCKUP_RECEIPTS
metadata.comment: Receipts for lockup transactions.

RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID.name: RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID
RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID.comment: Please note this column is being deprecated in favor of simply "receipt_id".

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

PREDECESSOR_ID.name: PREDECESSOR_ID
PREDECESSOR_ID.comment:
This is the account that was the immediate caller to the smart contract. If this is a simple transaction (no cross-contract calls) from alice.near to banana.near, the smart contract at banana.near considers Alice the predecessor. In this case, Alice would also be the signer.  
  
Source: https://docs.near.org/tutorials/crosswords/beginner/actions#predecessor-signer-and-current-account

RECEIVER_ID.name: RECEIVER_ID
RECEIVER_ID.comment:
The address of the NEAR account that the receipt was executed on.
Note, this differs from `tx_receiver` and is associated with the receipt within a transaction. It is often a contract on which a method is executed by the `signer_id`.

ACTIONS.name: ACTIONS
ACTIONS.comment: The actions executed during the receipt or transaction processing.

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp for when the block was inserted into the data warehouse by the Chainwalker.

_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.name: _PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER
_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Block number grouping for ingestion partition

ATLAS_SUPPLY_LOCKUP_RECEIPTS_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

_INVOCATION_ID.name: _INVOCATION_ID
_INVOCATION_ID.comment: A job ID to identify the run that last modified a record.

_MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: _MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
_MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment:
The timestamp at which the underlying record was last modified by an internal process.
This is used for incrementally loading based on when the source data was last modified.

metadata.name: STANDARD_NFT_MINT_S3
metadata.comment: This table records all the NFT Mints per the NEP171 standard, across various methods.

ACTION_ID.name: ACTION_ID
ACTION_ID.comment: The `action_id` is a concatenation of `receipt_id `and the index of the action within a receipt. This is unique for each record. The primary hash depends on the source of the action, as both a transaction and a receipt can process actions.

MINT_ACTION_ID.name: MINT_ACTION_ID
MINT_ACTION_ID.comment: Unique id for each standar mint event

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

METHOD_NAME.name: METHOD_NAME
METHOD_NAME.comment: Name of the method(s) approved for use. NULL if permission is FullAccess.

ARGS.name: ARGS
ARGS.comment: Decoded (where possible) arguments passed alongside the `method_name`.  If decoding failed, the table will contain the raw message.

DEPOSIT.name: DEPOSIT
DEPOSIT.comment:
Sum of all NEAR tokens transferred from the signing account to the receiver account during receipt processing. This includes tokens sent in a Transfer action(s), and tokens sent in a FunctionCall action(s) with a deposit attached.

In raw number format with 24 decimal places, to adjust divide by POW(10,24) or multiply by 1e-24.

TX_STATUS.name: TX_STATUS
TX_STATUS.comment: /// DEPRECATION WARNING /// Indicates whether the transaction failed or succeeded. This will be get deprecated in the future. Please use `tx_succeeded` instead.

RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID.name: RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID
RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID.comment: Please note this column is being deprecated in favor of simply "receipt_id".

RECEIVER_ID.name: RECEIVER_ID
RECEIVER_ID.comment:
The address of the NEAR account that the receipt was executed on.
Note, this differs from `tx_receiver` and is associated with the receipt within a transaction. It is often a contract on which a method is executed by the `signer_id`.

SIGNER_ID.name: SIGNER_ID
SIGNER_ID.comment: The signer is the account that originally signed the transaction that began the blockchain activity, which may or may not include cross-contract calls. If a function calls results in several cross-contract calls, think of the signer as the account that pushed over the first domino in that chain reaction.

TOKEN_ID.name: TOKEN_ID
TOKEN_ID.comment: The token ID for this NFT contract.

MEMO.name: MEMO
MEMO.comment: Memo: The log message within Near now includes an optional dictionary.

OWNER_PER_TX.name: OWNER_PER_TX
OWNER_PER_TX.comment: Number of different owners batch minting, `data` array's len in the LOG message

MINT_PER_TX.name: MINT_PER_TX
MINT_PER_TX.comment: Number of NFTs minted per `nft_mint` event called.

GAS_BURNT.name: GAS_BURNT
GAS_BURNT.comment:
The gas used in the object, whether it be the transaction, action or receipt. In the case of a curated model where this column is included alongside a transaction fee, this is the gas burnt during the individual action or receipt.

In raw number format with 16 decimal places, to adjust divide by POW(10,16) or multiply by 1e-16.

TRANSACTION_FEE.name: TRANSACTION_FEE
TRANSACTION_FEE.comment:
Total fee paid in NEAR to execute this transaction, which is a summation of all fees paid for actions and receipts within the transaction.

In raw number format, to adjust divide by POW(10,24)

_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.name: _PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER
_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Block number grouping for ingestion partition

IMPLIED_PRICE.name: IMPLIED_PRICE
IMPLIED_PRICE.comment: The implied price of the NFT is calculated by dividing the deposit by the number of mint events.

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp for when the block was inserted into the data warehouse by the Chainwalker.

STANDARD_NFT_MINT_ID.name: STANDARD_NFT_MINT_ID
STANDARD_NFT_MINT_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

_INVOCATION_ID.name: _INVOCATION_ID
_INVOCATION_ID.comment: A job ID to identify the run that last modified a record.

TX_SUCCEEDED.name: TX_SUCCEEDED
TX_SUCCEEDED.comment: Indicates whether the transaction failed or succeeded.

metadata.name: STAKING_EPOCHS
metadata.comment:
This table extracts epoch information for each pool from the logs of the reward receipts. 
Note, it appears that the epoch log is only recorded during an act of withdrawal or deposit by a delegator, anbd not on each epoch.

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

POOL_ID.name: POOL_ID
POOL_ID.comment: The unique id for the pool involved in the swap.

EPOCH_NUMBER.name: EPOCH_NUMBER
EPOCH_NUMBER.comment: The epoch number for this staking reward payout. Epoch increment in order every 43,200 blocks.

REWARD_TOKENS.name: REWARD_TOKENS
REWARD_TOKENS.comment: The total amount of tokens rewarded to the validator for this staking reward payout. Number is presented raw and not decimal adjusted in the fact table.

TOTAL_STAKED_BALANCE.name: TOTAL_STAKED_BALANCE
TOTAL_STAKED_BALANCE.comment: The total staked NEAR at the time of the reward payout. Number is presented raw and not decimal adjusted in the fact table.

TOTAL_STAKING_SHARES.name: TOTAL_STAKING_SHARES
TOTAL_STAKING_SHARES.comment: Total staking shares that comprise the staked balance of the pool. Number is presented raw and not decimal adjusted in the fact table.

LOG.name: LOG
LOG.comment: A single log extracted from a receipt.

_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.name: _PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER
_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Block number grouping for ingestion partition

_EPOCH_ID.name: _EPOCH_ID
_EPOCH_ID.comment: An internal identifier for the epoch. This is a hashed representation of the pool_id and epoch number.

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp for when the block was inserted into the data warehouse by the Chainwalker.

STAKING_EPOCHS_ID.name: STAKING_EPOCHS_ID
STAKING_EPOCHS_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

_INVOCATION_ID.name: _INVOCATION_ID
_INVOCATION_ID.comment: A job ID to identify the run that last modified a record.

metadata.name: API_NEARBLOCKS_FTS
metadata.comment: Holds data ingested from the Nearblocks Fungible Token API endpoint at: https://api.nearblocks.io/api-docs/#/FTs/get_v1_fts

DATE.name: DATE
DATE.comment: The date from the UTC timestamp when the API was called and the data for this token was ingested.

TOKEN.name: TOKEN
TOKEN.comment: The long-form name of the token.

TOKEN_CONTRACT.name: TOKEN_CONTRACT
TOKEN_CONTRACT.comment: The contract address corresponding to the token, view details of the contract by following this address on the chain explorer.

TOKEN_DATA.name: TOKEN_DATA
TOKEN_DATA.comment: A JSON dictionary containing metadata about the token, such as past 24h volume and transfers, number of holders, etc.

DECIMALS.name: DECIMALS
DECIMALS.comment: The number of decimals a raw number should be divided by to decimal adjust. For example, bridged tokens from Ethereum typically use 18 decimals so a raw (unadjusted chain number) of 1000000000000000000 represents 1.

SYMBOL.name: SYMBOL
SYMBOL.comment: The shorthand symbol for the token, as used by common price feeds and exchanges.

PROVIDER.name: PROVIDER
PROVIDER.comment: The source or provider of the data in the record.

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp for when the block was inserted into the data warehouse by the Chainwalker.

_RES_ID.name: _RES_ID
_RES_ID.comment: The response ID which is a concatenation between the epoch timestamp and an identifier relevant to the data ingested, such as contract.

API_NEARBLOCKS_FTS_ID.name: API_NEARBLOCKS_FTS_ID
API_NEARBLOCKS_FTS_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

_INVOCATION_ID.name: _INVOCATION_ID
_INVOCATION_ID.comment: A job ID to identify the run that last modified a record.

metadata.name: BRIDGE_ALLBRIDGE
metadata.comment: Extracts data from actions table to build a view of bridge activity through the Allbridge.

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

TOKEN_ADDRESS.name: TOKEN_ADDRESS
TOKEN_ADDRESS.comment: The contract address corresponding to the token, view details of the contract by following this address on the chain explorer.

AMOUNT_RAW.name: AMOUNT_RAW
AMOUNT_RAW.comment: An unadjusted amount (of tokens, price, etc.) for the relevant record. This is the number as it appears on chain and is not decimal adjusted.

AMOUNT_FEE_RAW.name: AMOUNT_FEE_RAW
AMOUNT_FEE_RAW.comment: An unadjusted amount (of tokens, price, etc.) for the relevant record. This is the number as it appears on chain and is not decimal adjusted.

MEMO.name: MEMO
MEMO.comment: Memo: The log message within Near now includes an optional dictionary.

DESTINATION_ADDRESS.name: DESTINATION_ADDRESS
DESTINATION_ADDRESS.comment: The address tokens are being sent to, in a transfer or bridge transaction.

SOURCE_ADDRESS.name: SOURCE_ADDRESS
SOURCE_ADDRESS.comment:
The originating address from which tokens are being sent, in a transfer or bridge transaction.  

This addressed is ascertained as best as possible, but may be null depending on the available information.  

Example 1 - tx CzPRdoiU74bo9yqqt9fCP5Xdab2mPVqxr9gU5xeaQA4a stores just "signer_public_key 0x0x1589…f694" on chain.  

Example 2 - tx FA7tQeGQnhminbas28YgaizwmT4vnEUiCr3vdXmN3Xjn does not log any signing address at all.

DESTINATION_CHAIN_ID.name: DESTINATION_CHAIN_ID
DESTINATION_CHAIN_ID.comment:
Chain ID as defined by the corresponding bridge.

Wormhole Portal Bridge https://docs.wormhole.com/wormhole/reference/constants

Multichain Bridge https://docs.multichain.org/getting-started/introduction/supported-chains

Allbridge Classic https://github.com/allbridge-io/allbridge-contract-docs?tab=readme-ov-file#blockchain-ids

SOURCE_CHAIN_ID.name: SOURCE_CHAIN_ID
SOURCE_CHAIN_ID.comment:
Chain ID as defined by the corresponding bridge.

Wormhole Portal Bridge https://docs.wormhole.com/wormhole/reference/constants

Multichain Bridge https://docs.multichain.org/getting-started/introduction/supported-chains

Allbridge Classic https://github.com/allbridge-io/allbridge-contract-docs?tab=readme-ov-file#blockchain-ids

ARGS.name: ARGS
ARGS.comment: Decoded (where possible) arguments passed alongside the `method_name`.  If decoding failed, the table will contain the raw message.

RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED.name: RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED
RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED.comment: A boolean indicating whether the receipt was successfully processed, based on the presence of a Failure message.

METHOD_NAME.name: METHOD_NAME

DIRECTION.name: DIRECTION

Note - for the Rainbow bridge, inbound means Near or Aurora as tokens pass through Near to get to Aurora.

PLATFORM.name: PLATFORM

metadata.name: FACT_DECODED_ACTIONS
metadata.comment: Deprecating Soon - please cease using this table by February 1, 2025.

ACTION_ID_HORIZON.name: ACTION_ID_HORIZON
ACTION_ID_HORIZON.comment: The `action_id` is a concatenation of `receipt_id `and the index of the action within a receipt. This is unique for each record. The primary hash depends on the source of the action, as both a transaction and a receipt can process actions.

RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID.name: RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID
RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID.comment: Please note this column is being deprecated in favor of simply "receipt_id".

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

METHOD_NAME.name: METHOD_NAME
METHOD_NAME.comment: Name of the method(s) approved for use. NULL if permission is FullAccess.

ARGS.name: ARGS
ARGS.comment: Decoded (where possible) arguments passed alongside the `method_name`.  If decoding failed, the table will contain the raw message.

DEPOSIT.name: DEPOSIT
DEPOSIT.comment:
Sum of all NEAR tokens transferred from the signing account to the receiver account during receipt processing. This includes tokens sent in a Transfer action(s), and tokens sent in a FunctionCall action(s) with a deposit attached.

In raw number format with 24 decimal places, to adjust divide by POW(10,24) or multiply by 1e-24.

ATTACHED_GAS.name: ATTACHED_GAS
ATTACHED_GAS.comment: Units of gas (figure is not decimal adjusted, 10^12) attached to the transaction (this is often higher than 'Gas Used').

RECEIVER_ID.name: RECEIVER_ID
RECEIVER_ID.comment:
The address of the NEAR account that the receipt was executed on.
Note, this differs from `tx_receiver` and is associated with the receipt within a transaction. It is often a contract on which a method is executed by the `signer_id`.

SIGNER_ID.name: SIGNER_ID
SIGNER_ID.comment: The signer is the account that originally signed the transaction that began the blockchain activity, which may or may not include cross-contract calls. If a function calls results in several cross-contract calls, think of the signer as the account that pushed over the first domino in that chain reaction.

RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED.name: RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED
RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED.comment: A boolean indicating whether the receipt was successfully processed, based on the presence of a Failure message.

FACT_DECODED_ACTIONS_ID.name: FACT_DECODED_ACTIONS_ID
FACT_DECODED_ACTIONS_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

metadata.name: EZ_CORE_METRICS_HOURLY
metadata.comment: A convenience table that aggregates block and transaction related metrics using various aggregate functions such as SUM, COUNT, MIN and MAX from the fact_transactions table, on an hourly basis. Stats for the current hour will be updated as new data arrives.

metadata.name: ACTIONS_EVENTS_FUNCTION_CALL_S3
metadata.comment: This table extracts all FunctionCall events from actions and decodes the arguments for easy use.

ACTION_ID.name: ACTION_ID
ACTION_ID.comment: The `action_id` is a concatenation of `receipt_id `and the index of the action within a receipt. This is unique for each record. The primary hash depends on the source of the action, as both a transaction and a receipt can process actions.

RECEIVER_ID.name: RECEIVER_ID
RECEIVER_ID.comment:
The address of the NEAR account that the receipt was executed on.
Note, this differs from `tx_receiver` and is associated with the receipt within a transaction. It is often a contract on which a method is executed by the `signer_id`.

PREDECESSOR_ID.name: PREDECESSOR_ID
PREDECESSOR_ID.comment:
This is the account that was the immediate caller to the smart contract. If this is a simple transaction (no cross-contract calls) from alice.near to banana.near, the smart contract at banana.near considers Alice the predecessor. In this case, Alice would also be the signer.  
  
Source: https://docs.near.org/tutorials/crosswords/beginner/actions#predecessor-signer-and-current-account

SIGNER_ID.name: SIGNER_ID
SIGNER_ID.comment: The signer is the account that originally signed the transaction that began the blockchain activity, which may or may not include cross-contract calls. If a function calls results in several cross-contract calls, think of the signer as the account that pushed over the first domino in that chain reaction.

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

ACTION_NAME.name: ACTION_NAME
ACTION_NAME.comment: The name of the action performed.

METHOD_NAME.name: METHOD_NAME
METHOD_NAME.comment: Name of the method(s) approved for use. NULL if permission is FullAccess.

ARGS.name: ARGS
ARGS.comment: Decoded (where possible) arguments passed alongside the `method_name`.  If decoding failed, the table will contain the raw message.

DEPOSIT.name: DEPOSIT
DEPOSIT.comment:
Sum of all NEAR tokens transferred from the signing account to the receiver account during receipt processing. This includes tokens sent in a Transfer action(s), and tokens sent in a FunctionCall action(s) with a deposit attached.

In raw number format with 24 decimal places, to adjust divide by POW(10,24) or multiply by 1e-24.

ATTACHED_GAS.name: ATTACHED_GAS
ATTACHED_GAS.comment: Units of gas (figure is not decimal adjusted, 10^12) attached to the transaction (this is often higher than 'Gas Used').

RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED.name: RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED
RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED.comment: A boolean indicating whether the receipt was successfully processed, based on the presence of a Failure message.

_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.name: _PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER
_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Block number grouping for ingestion partition

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp for when the block was inserted into the data warehouse by the Chainwalker.

ACTIONS_EVENTS_FUNCTION_CALL_ID.name: ACTIONS_EVENTS_FUNCTION_CALL_ID
ACTIONS_EVENTS_FUNCTION_CALL_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

_INVOCATION_ID.name: _INVOCATION_ID
_INVOCATION_ID.comment: A job ID to identify the run that last modified a record.

metadata.name: FACT_RECEIPTS
metadata.comment: Decoded and flattened transaction receipts from the block shards.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

RECEIPT_ID.name: RECEIPT_ID
RECEIPT_ID.comment: The identifying hash for a receipt.

RECEIPT_OUTCOME_ID.name: RECEIPT_OUTCOME_ID
RECEIPT_OUTCOME_ID.comment: Unique identifier(s) (array) of the remaining receipt outcomes for this transaction. This list will not include the record's own receipt id, but will list the subsequent receipts within the transaction.

RECEIVER_ID.name: RECEIVER_ID
RECEIVER_ID.comment:
The address of the NEAR account that the receipt was executed on.
Note, this differs from `tx_receiver` and is associated with the receipt within a transaction. It is often a contract on which a method is executed by the `signer_id`.

PREDECESSOR_ID.name: PREDECESSOR_ID
PREDECESSOR_ID.comment:
This is the account that was the immediate caller to the smart contract. If this is a simple transaction (no cross-contract calls) from alice.near to banana.near, the smart contract at banana.near considers Alice the predecessor. In this case, Alice would also be the signer.  
  
Source: https://docs.near.org/tutorials/crosswords/beginner/actions#predecessor-signer-and-current-account

ACTIONS.name: ACTIONS
ACTIONS.comment: The actions executed during the receipt or transaction processing.

OUTCOME.name: OUTCOME
OUTCOME.comment: The outcome of the transaction, action, or receipt that was executed in the shard.

GAS_BURNT.name: GAS_BURNT
GAS_BURNT.comment:
The gas used in the object, whether it be the transaction, action or receipt. In the case of a curated model where this column is included alongside a transaction fee, this is the gas burnt during the individual action or receipt.

In raw number format with 16 decimal places, to adjust divide by POW(10,16) or multiply by 1e-16.

METADATA.name: METADATA
METADATA.comment: Metadata (object) for this transaction. May be null, especially for Near Social Widgets.

FACT_RECEIPTS_ID.name: FACT_RECEIPTS_ID
FACT_RECEIPTS_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

metadata.name: EZ_NFT_CONTRACT_METRICS
metadata.comment:
Deprecating Soon - these tables previously supported NEAR Atlas which is no longer live. These tables will be removed on Febreary 1, 2025.
Please cease using these tables.

RECEIVER_ID.name: RECEIVER_ID
RECEIVER_ID.comment: { { doc('receiver_id')}}

TOKENS.name: TOKENS
TOKENS.comment: "The count of unique tokens that have been received by the receiver_id."

TRANSFERS_24H.name: TRANSFERS_24H
TRANSFERS_24H.comment: The count of 'nft_transfer' transactions that occurred in the last 24 hours.

TRANSFERS_3D.name: TRANSFERS_3D
TRANSFERS_3D.comment: The count of 'nft_transfer' transactions that occurred in the last 3 days.

OWNERS.name: OWNERS
OWNERS.comment: "The count of distinct owners that have interacted with the receiver's tokens."

TRANSACTIONS.name: TRANSACTIONS
TRANSACTIONS.comment: The `tx_count` taken from block headers. The number of transactions included in the block.

MINTS.name: MINTS
MINTS.comment: "The count of transactions where the method_name indicates a minting event rather than a transfer."

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

metadata.name: STAKING_ACTIONS_V2
metadata.comment:
An updated version of the staking actions table which looks at all logs, instead of just the first receipt.
There are four actions taken when staking: staking->deposit->unstaking->withdraw.

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID.name: RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID
RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID.comment: Please note this column is being deprecated in favor of simply "receipt_id".

RECEIVER_ID.name: RECEIVER_ID
RECEIVER_ID.comment:
The address of the NEAR account that the receipt was executed on.
Note, this differs from `tx_receiver` and is associated with the receipt within a transaction. It is often a contract on which a method is executed by the `signer_id`.

SIGNER_ID.name: SIGNER_ID
SIGNER_ID.comment: The signer is the account that originally signed the transaction that began the blockchain activity, which may or may not include cross-contract calls. If a function calls results in several cross-contract calls, think of the signer as the account that pushed over the first domino in that chain reaction.

PREDECESSOR_ID.name: PREDECESSOR_ID
PREDECESSOR_ID.comment:
This is the account that was the immediate caller to the smart contract. If this is a simple transaction (no cross-contract calls) from alice.near to banana.near, the smart contract at banana.near considers Alice the predecessor. In this case, Alice would also be the signer.  
  
Source: https://docs.near.org/tutorials/crosswords/beginner/actions#predecessor-signer-and-current-account

LOG.name: LOG
LOG.comment: A single log extracted from a receipt.

LOG_SIGNER_ID.name: LOG_SIGNER_ID
LOG_SIGNER_ID.comment: The NEAR address indicated in the log.

ACTION.name: ACTION
ACTION.comment: The staking action performed in this transaction. Can be `"Stake"` or `"Unstake"` if in the deprecating `dim_staking_actions` table, or `staking`, `unstaking`, `deposited`, `withdrawing` if in the new `fact_staking_actions` table. These method names are taken directly from the log in which they occur.

AMOUNT_RAW.name: AMOUNT_RAW
AMOUNT_RAW.comment: An unadjusted amount (of tokens, price, etc.) for the relevant record. This is the number as it appears on chain and is not decimal adjusted.

AMOUNT_ADJ.name: AMOUNT_ADJ
AMOUNT_ADJ.comment: A decimal adjusted amount (of tokens, price, etc.).

DECIMALS.name: DECIMALS
DECIMALS.comment: The number of decimals a raw number should be divided by to decimal adjust. For example, bridged tokens from Ethereum typically use 18 decimals so a raw (unadjusted chain number) of 1000000000000000000 represents 1.

_LOG_SIGNER_ID_MATCH.name: _LOG_SIGNER_ID_MATCH
_LOG_SIGNER_ID_MATCH.comment: A boolean indicating whether the signer_id of the log matches the signer_id of the transaction. Internal / silver column only.

_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.name: _PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER
_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Block number grouping for ingestion partition

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp for when the block was inserted into the data warehouse by the Chainwalker.

STAKING_ACTIONS_V2_ID.name: STAKING_ACTIONS_V2_ID
STAKING_ACTIONS_V2_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

_INVOCATION_ID.name: _INVOCATION_ID
_INVOCATION_ID.comment: A job ID to identify the run that last modified a record.

metadata.name: DIM_NFT_SERIES_METADATA
metadata.comment: NFT Series-level metadata provided by the Pagoda NFT endpoint.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the deployed contract for the token, nft, or other smart contract.

SERIES_ID.name: SERIES_ID
SERIES_ID.comment: The series, or collection, id for a NFT project. Many platforms issues NFT collections from a marketplace contract. For example, the contract `x.paras.near` is the Paras NFT Marketplace and mints NFTs from this master-contract. A token ID 1234:123 indicates that the NFT is token `123` within series `1234`.

METADATA_ID.name: METADATA_ID
METADATA_ID.comment: A MD5 Hash of an NFT contract and series ID, providing an unique indicator for collecting NFT Series Metadata.

CONTRACT_METADATA.name: CONTRACT_METADATA
CONTRACT_METADATA.comment: Token contract metadata from the Pagoda API. Should correspond with the metadata provided by the Nearblocks API.

TOKEN_METADATA.name: TOKEN_METADATA
TOKEN_METADATA.comment: NFT token-level metadata for a NFT series / collection.

DIM_NFT_SERIES_METADATA_ID.name: DIM_NFT_SERIES_METADATA_ID
DIM_NFT_SERIES_METADATA_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

ACTION_ID.comment: The `action_id` is a concatenation of `receipt_id `and the index of the action within a receipt. This is unique for each record. The primary hash depends on the source of the action, as both a transaction and a receipt can process actions.

ACTIONS.name: ACTIONS

Deposit: user is depositing funds to be used for lending

metadata.name: DECODED_ACTIONS
metadata.comment: Decoded FunctionCall events for receipts where the contract social.near was called.

ACTION_ID_SOCIAL.name: ACTION_ID_SOCIAL
ACTION_ID_SOCIAL.comment: The `action_id` is a concatenation of `receipt_id `and the index of the action within a receipt. This is unique for each record. The primary hash depends on the source of the action, as both a transaction and a receipt can process actions.

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

SIGNER_ID.name: SIGNER_ID
SIGNER_ID.comment: The signer is the account that originally signed the transaction that began the blockchain activity, which may or may not include cross-contract calls. If a function calls results in several cross-contract calls, think of the signer as the account that pushed over the first domino in that chain reaction.

PREDECESSOR_ID.name: PREDECESSOR_ID
PREDECESSOR_ID.comment:
This is the account that was the immediate caller to the smart contract. If this is a simple transaction (no cross-contract calls) from alice.near to banana.near, the smart contract at banana.near considers Alice the predecessor. In this case, Alice would also be the signer.  
  
Source: https://docs.near.org/tutorials/crosswords/beginner/actions#predecessor-signer-and-current-account

NODE.name: NODE
NODE.comment:
A branch of the data object. 

In the case of NEAR Social schema, node is being used to denote the top-level key of the data object.

NODE_DATA.name: NODE_DATA
NODE_DATA.comment: The data object that the top level node contains.

_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.name: _PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER

metadata.name: BLOCKS_COMPLETENESS
metadata.comment: Observability model that queries the blocks table at a designated interval to record and track the completeness of the data.

TEST_NAME.name: TEST_NAME
TEST_NAME.comment: The name of the test for observability models.

MIN_BLOCK.name: MIN_BLOCK
MIN_BLOCK.comment: The minimum block tested in the range of the observability test run.

MAX_BLOCK.name: MAX_BLOCK
MAX_BLOCK.comment: The maximum block tested in the range of the observability test run.

MIN_BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: MIN_BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
MIN_BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The block timestamp of the min block tested in the observability run.

MAX_BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: MAX_BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
MAX_BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The block timestamp of the max block tested in the observability run.

BLOCKS_TESTED.name: BLOCKS_TESTED
BLOCKS_TESTED.comment: The number of blocks tested in the observability run.

BLOCKS_IMPACTED_COUNT.name: BLOCKS_IMPACTED_COUNT
BLOCKS_IMPACTED_COUNT.comment:
The number of blocks impacted during the observability run.  
Impacted is defined as missing or having bad data.

BLOCKS_IMPACTED_ARRAY.name: BLOCKS_IMPACTED_ARRAY
BLOCKS_IMPACTED_ARRAY.comment: An array of the impacted blocks during the observability run.

TEST_TIMESTAMP.name: TEST_TIMESTAMP
TEST_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp of the observability test run.

metadata.name: FACT_DECODED_ACTIONS
metadata.comment: Decoded FunctionCall events for receipts where the contract social.near was called.

ACTION_ID_SOCIAL.name: ACTION_ID_SOCIAL
ACTION_ID_SOCIAL.comment: The `action_id` is a concatenation of `receipt_id `and the index of the action within a receipt. This is unique for each record. The primary hash depends on the source of the action, as both a transaction and a receipt can process actions.

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

SIGNER_ID.name: SIGNER_ID
SIGNER_ID.comment: The signer is the account that originally signed the transaction that began the blockchain activity, which may or may not include cross-contract calls. If a function calls results in several cross-contract calls, think of the signer as the account that pushed over the first domino in that chain reaction.

PREDECESSOR_ID.name: PREDECESSOR_ID
PREDECESSOR_ID.comment:
This is the account that was the immediate caller to the smart contract. If this is a simple transaction (no cross-contract calls) from alice.near to banana.near, the smart contract at banana.near considers Alice the predecessor. In this case, Alice would also be the signer.  
  
Source: https://docs.near.org/tutorials/crosswords/beginner/actions#predecessor-signer-and-current-account

NODE.name: NODE
NODE.comment:
A branch of the data object. 

In the case of NEAR Social schema, node is being used to denote the top-level key of the data object.

NODE_DATA.name: NODE_DATA
NODE_DATA.comment: The data object that the top level node contains.

FACT_DECODED_ACTIONS_ID.name: FACT_DECODED_ACTIONS_ID
FACT_DECODED_ACTIONS_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

ASSET_ID.comment: The unique identifier representing the asset.

DECIMALS.name: DECIMALS

BLOCKCHAIN.name: BLOCKCHAIN
BLOCKCHAIN.comment: The Blockchain, Network, or Platform for this asset.

IS_NATIVE.name: IS_NATIVE

IS_DEPRECATED.name: IS_DEPRECATED

metadata.name: FACT_ACTIONS_EVENTS
metadata.comment:
Deprecating soon! Please migrate to using the new `core__ez_actions` table.
This view will be removed in Q1 2025.

ACTION_ID.name: ACTION_ID
ACTION_ID.comment: The `action_id` is a concatenation of `receipt_id `and the index of the action within a receipt. This is unique for each record. The primary hash depends on the source of the action, as both a transaction and a receipt can process actions.

RECEIPT_ID.name: RECEIPT_ID
RECEIPT_ID.comment: The identifying hash for a receipt.

PREDECESSOR_ID.name: PREDECESSOR_ID
PREDECESSOR_ID.comment:
This is the account that was the immediate caller to the smart contract. If this is a simple transaction (no cross-contract calls) from alice.near to banana.near, the smart contract at banana.near considers Alice the predecessor. In this case, Alice would also be the signer.  
  
Source: https://docs.near.org/tutorials/crosswords/beginner/actions#predecessor-signer-and-current-account

RECEIVER_ID.name: RECEIVER_ID
RECEIVER_ID.comment:
The address of the NEAR account that the receipt was executed on.
Note, this differs from `tx_receiver` and is associated with the receipt within a transaction. It is often a contract on which a method is executed by the `signer_id`.

SIGNER_ID.name: SIGNER_ID
SIGNER_ID.comment: The signer is the account that originally signed the transaction that began the blockchain activity, which may or may not include cross-contract calls. If a function calls results in several cross-contract calls, think of the signer as the account that pushed over the first domino in that chain reaction.

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

ACTION_INDEX.name: ACTION_INDEX
ACTION_INDEX.comment: The index of the current `action_name` and `action_data` in the order in which it appeared in the transaction.

ACTION_NAME.name: ACTION_NAME
ACTION_NAME.comment: The name of the action performed.

ACTION_DATA.name: ACTION_DATA
ACTION_DATA.comment: A JSON object containing the argument data that was called by the `action_event`, if any.

RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED.name: RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED
RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED.comment: A boolean indicating whether the receipt was successfully processed, based on the presence of a Failure message.

FACT_ACTIONS_EVENTS_ID.name: FACT_ACTIONS_EVENTS_ID
FACT_ACTIONS_EVENTS_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

metadata.name: FACT_BLOCKS
metadata.comment: This table records all the blocks of Near blockchain.

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

BLOCK_AUTHOR.name: BLOCK_AUTHOR
BLOCK_AUTHOR.comment: The `block_author` taken from block headers. Validators of the blockchain.

HEADER.name: HEADER
HEADER.comment: A json column containing header information from the object: block or chunk.

BLOCK_CHALLENGES_RESULT.name: BLOCK_CHALLENGES_RESULT
BLOCK_CHALLENGES_RESULT.comment: The block_challenges_result taken from block headers.

BLOCK_CHALLENGES_ROOT.name: BLOCK_CHALLENGES_ROOT
BLOCK_CHALLENGES_ROOT.comment: The block_challenges_root taken from block headers.

CHUNK_HEADERS_ROOT.name: CHUNK_HEADERS_ROOT
CHUNK_HEADERS_ROOT.comment: The chunk_headers_root taken from block headers.

CHUNK_TX_ROOT.name: CHUNK_TX_ROOT
CHUNK_TX_ROOT.comment: The chunk_tx_root taken from block headers.

CHUNK_MASK.name: CHUNK_MASK
CHUNK_MASK.comment: The chunk_mask taken from block headers.

CHUNK_RECEIPTS_ROOT.name: CHUNK_RECEIPTS_ROOT
CHUNK_RECEIPTS_ROOT.comment: The chunk_receipts_root taken from block headers.

CHUNKS.name: CHUNKS
CHUNKS.comment: The chunks taken from block headers. Chunk is an aggregation of transactions which are executed within a particular shard.

CHUNKS_INCLUDED.name: CHUNKS_INCLUDED
CHUNKS_INCLUDED.comment: The chunks_included taken from block headers.

EPOCH_ID.name: EPOCH_ID
EPOCH_ID.comment: The epoch_id taken from block headers.

EPOCH_SYNC_DATA_HASH.name: EPOCH_SYNC_DATA_HASH
EPOCH_SYNC_DATA_HASH.comment: The epoch_sync_data_hash taken from block headers, if available.

GAS_PRICE.name: GAS_PRICE
GAS_PRICE.comment: The gas_price taken from block headers, number is not decimal adjusted.

LAST_DS_FINAL_BLOCK.name: LAST_DS_FINAL_BLOCK
LAST_DS_FINAL_BLOCK.comment: The last_ds_final_block taken from block headers.

LAST_FINAL_BLOCK.name: LAST_FINAL_BLOCK
LAST_FINAL_BLOCK.comment: The last_final_block taken from block headers.

LATEST_PROTOCOL_VERSION.name: LATEST_PROTOCOL_VERSION
LATEST_PROTOCOL_VERSION.comment: The latest_protocol_version taken from block headers.

NEXT_BP_HASH.name: NEXT_BP_HASH
NEXT_BP_HASH.comment: The next_bp_hash taken from block headers.

NEXT_EPOCH_ID.name: NEXT_EPOCH_ID
NEXT_EPOCH_ID.comment: The next_epoch_id taken from block headers.

OUTCOME_ROOT.name: OUTCOME_ROOT
OUTCOME_ROOT.comment: The outcome_root taken from block headers.

PREV_HASH.name: PREV_HASH
PREV_HASH.comment: The prev_hash taken from block headers.

PREV_HEIGHT.name: PREV_HEIGHT
PREV_HEIGHT.comment: The prev_height taken from block headers.

PREV_STATE_ROOT.name: PREV_STATE_ROOT
PREV_STATE_ROOT.comment: The prev_state_root taken from block headers.

RANDOM_VALUE.name: RANDOM_VALUE
RANDOM_VALUE.comment: The random_value taken from block headers.

RENT_PAID.name: RENT_PAID
RENT_PAID.comment: The rent_paid taken from block headers.

SIGNATURE.name: SIGNATURE
SIGNATURE.comment: The signature taken from block headers.

TOTAL_SUPPLY.name: TOTAL_SUPPLY
TOTAL_SUPPLY.comment: The total_supply taken from block headers.

VALIDATOR_PROPOSALS.name: VALIDATOR_PROPOSALS
VALIDATOR_PROPOSALS.comment: The validator_proposals taken from block headers.

VALIDATOR_REWARD.name: VALIDATOR_REWARD
VALIDATOR_REWARD.comment: The validator_reward taken from block headers.

FACT_BLOCKS_ID.name: FACT_BLOCKS_ID
FACT_BLOCKS_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: STREAMLINE_RECEIPTS
metadata.comment:
Singular receipt objects with the shard id and chunk hash from which it was included. 
These receipts are only action receipts from the execution outcome of the shard.

RECEIPT_ID.name: RECEIPT_ID
RECEIPT_ID.comment: The identifying hash for a receipt.

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

SHARD_ID.name: SHARD_ID
SHARD_ID.comment: The id for this shard, which is block_id plus shard number

RECEIPT_INDEX.name: RECEIPT_INDEX
RECEIPT_INDEX.comment: Deprecation notice! Please note this column will be removed from the receipts tables. For more context on NEAR Receipts, please see the announcement in Discord here: https://discord.com/channels/784442203187314689/992655061517881385/1098015182103523408

RECEIPT.name: RECEIPT
RECEIPT.comment: A single receipt produced as a byproduct of the actions taken within a transaction.

EXECUTION_OUTCOME.name: EXECUTION_OUTCOME
EXECUTION_OUTCOME.comment: The outcome of the transaction, action, or receipt that was executed in the shard.

RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED.name: RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED
RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED.comment: A boolean indicating whether the receipt was successfully processed, based on the presence of a Failure message.

ERROR_TYPE_0.name: ERROR_TYPE_0
ERROR_TYPE_0.comment: The top-level error type, which likely pertains to the type of object that failed. For example `ActionError` when a action failed to execute for some reason.

ERROR_TYPE_1.name: ERROR_TYPE_1
ERROR_TYPE_1.comment: The second level of error type when a receipt has failed. This pertains to the scope in which the error occurred, such as `FunctionCallError`.

ERROR_TYPE_2.name: ERROR_TYPE_2
ERROR_TYPE_2.comment: The third level of error type which actually pertains to the type of failure. This is commonly `ExecutionError`.

ERROR_MESSAGE.name: ERROR_MESSAGE
ERROR_MESSAGE.comment: The error message, if any, associated with the failure.

OUTCOME_RECEIPTS.name: OUTCOME_RECEIPTS
OUTCOME_RECEIPTS.comment: Unique identifier(s) (array) of the remaining receipt outcomes for this transaction. This list will not include the record's own receipt id, but will list the subsequent receipts within the transaction.

RECEIVER_ID.name: RECEIVER_ID
RECEIVER_ID.comment:
The address of the NEAR account that the receipt was executed on.
Note, this differs from `tx_receiver` and is associated with the receipt within a transaction. It is often a contract on which a method is executed by the `signer_id`.

SIGNER_ID.name: SIGNER_ID
SIGNER_ID.comment: The signer is the account that originally signed the transaction that began the blockchain activity, which may or may not include cross-contract calls. If a function calls results in several cross-contract calls, think of the signer as the account that pushed over the first domino in that chain reaction.

RECEIPT_TYPE.name: RECEIPT_TYPE
RECEIPT_TYPE.comment: The type of receipt, as taken from the actions executed during the receipt. Options, presently, are Action or Data.

_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.name: _PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER
_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Block number grouping for ingestion partition

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp for when the block was inserted into the data warehouse by the Chainwalker.

STREAMLINE_RECEIPTS_ID.name: STREAMLINE_RECEIPTS_ID
STREAMLINE_RECEIPTS_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

metadata.name: ACTIONS_EVENTS_S3
metadata.comment: This table extracts all action events from a receipt and stores the argument data under action_data.

ACTION_ID.name: ACTION_ID
ACTION_ID.comment: The `action_id` is a concatenation of `receipt_id `and the index of the action within a receipt. This is unique for each record. The primary hash depends on the source of the action, as both a transaction and a receipt can process actions.

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID.name: RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID
RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID.comment: Please note this column is being deprecated in favor of simply "receipt_id".

PREDECESSOR_ID.name: PREDECESSOR_ID
PREDECESSOR_ID.comment:
This is the account that was the immediate caller to the smart contract. If this is a simple transaction (no cross-contract calls) from alice.near to banana.near, the smart contract at banana.near considers Alice the predecessor. In this case, Alice would also be the signer.  
  
Source: https://docs.near.org/tutorials/crosswords/beginner/actions#predecessor-signer-and-current-account

RECEIVER_ID.name: RECEIVER_ID
RECEIVER_ID.comment:
The address of the NEAR account that the receipt was executed on.
Note, this differs from `tx_receiver` and is associated with the receipt within a transaction. It is often a contract on which a method is executed by the `signer_id`.

SIGNER_ID.name: SIGNER_ID
SIGNER_ID.comment: The signer is the account that originally signed the transaction that began the blockchain activity, which may or may not include cross-contract calls. If a function calls results in several cross-contract calls, think of the signer as the account that pushed over the first domino in that chain reaction.

ACTION_INDEX.name: ACTION_INDEX
ACTION_INDEX.comment: The index of the current `action_name` and `action_data` in the order in which it appeared in the transaction.

ACTION_NAME.name: ACTION_NAME
ACTION_NAME.comment: The name of the action performed.

ACTION_DATA.name: ACTION_DATA
ACTION_DATA.comment: A JSON object containing the argument data that was called by the `action_event`, if any.

RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED.name: RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED
RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED.comment: A boolean indicating whether the receipt was successfully processed, based on the presence of a Failure message.

_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.name: _PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER
_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Block number grouping for ingestion partition

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp for when the block was inserted into the data warehouse by the Chainwalker.

ACTIONS_EVENTS_ID.name: ACTIONS_EVENTS_ID
ACTIONS_EVENTS_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

_INVOCATION_ID.name: _INVOCATION_ID
_INVOCATION_ID.comment: A job ID to identify the run that last modified a record.

metadata.name: DIM_ADDRESS_LABELS
metadata.comment: "The labels table is a store of one-to-one address identifiers, or an address name. Labels are broken out into a "type" (such as cex, dex, dapp, games, etc.) and a "subtype" (ex: contract_deployer, hot_wallet, token_contract, etc.) in order to help classify each address name into similar groups. Our labels are sourced from many different places, but can primarily be grouped into two categories: automatic and manual. Automatic labels are continuously labeled based on certain criteria, such as a known contract deploying another contract, behavior based algorithms for finding deposit wallets, and consistent data pulls of custom protocol APIs. Manual labels are done periodically to find addresses that cannot be found programatically such as finding new protocol addresses, centralized exchange hot wallets, or trending addresses. Labels can also be added by our community by using our add-a-label tool (https://science.flipsidecrypto.xyz/add-a-label/) or on-chain with near (https://near.social/lord1.near/widget/Form) and are reviewed by our labels team. A label can be removed by our labels team if it is found to be incorrect or no longer relevant; this generally will only happen for mislabeled deposit wallets."

SYSTEM_CREATED_AT.name: SYSTEM_CREATED_AT
SYSTEM_CREATED_AT.comment: The timestamp for when the label was created by the Flipside label tool.

BLOCKCHAIN.name: BLOCKCHAIN
BLOCKCHAIN.comment: The shorthand symbol for the token, as used by common price feeds and exchanges.

ADDRESS.name: ADDRESS
ADDRESS.comment: The NEAR address that corresponds with a label.

ADDRESS_NAME.name: ADDRESS_NAME
ADDRESS_NAME.comment: The name attributed to the address.

LABEL_TYPE.name: LABEL_TYPE
LABEL_TYPE.comment: A high-level category describing the addresses main function or ownership.

LABEL_SUBTYPE.name: LABEL_SUBTYPE
LABEL_SUBTYPE.comment: A sub-category nested within label type providing further detail.

L1_LABEL.name: L1_LABEL
L1_LABEL.comment: Deprecating soon. This is the same column label type, but is being kept for compatibility until 3/1/2023. TODO - deprecate.

L2_LABEL.name: L2_LABEL
L2_LABEL.comment: Deprecating soon. This is the same column label subtype, but is being kept for compatibility until 3/1/2023. TODO - deprecate.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

metadata.name: DECODED_ACTIONS
metadata.comment: Decoded FunctionCall events for receipts where the contract nearhorizon.near was called.

ACTION_ID_HORIZON.name: ACTION_ID_HORIZON
ACTION_ID_HORIZON.comment: The `action_id` is a concatenation of `receipt_id `and the index of the action within a receipt. This is unique for each record. The primary hash depends on the source of the action, as both a transaction and a receipt can process actions.

RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID.name: RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID
RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID.comment: Please note this column is being deprecated in favor of simply "receipt_id".

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

METHOD_NAME.name: METHOD_NAME
METHOD_NAME.comment: Name of the method(s) approved for use. NULL if permission is FullAccess.

ARGS.name: ARGS
ARGS.comment: Decoded (where possible) arguments passed alongside the `method_name`.  If decoding failed, the table will contain the raw message.

DEPOSIT.name: DEPOSIT
DEPOSIT.comment:
Sum of all NEAR tokens transferred from the signing account to the receiver account during receipt processing. This includes tokens sent in a Transfer action(s), and tokens sent in a FunctionCall action(s) with a deposit attached.

In raw number format with 24 decimal places, to adjust divide by POW(10,24) or multiply by 1e-24.

ATTACHED_GAS.name: ATTACHED_GAS
ATTACHED_GAS.comment: Units of gas (figure is not decimal adjusted, 10^12) attached to the transaction (this is often higher than 'Gas Used').

RECEIVER_ID.name: RECEIVER_ID
RECEIVER_ID.comment:
The address of the NEAR account that the receipt was executed on.
Note, this differs from `tx_receiver` and is associated with the receipt within a transaction. It is often a contract on which a method is executed by the `signer_id`.

SIGNER_ID.name: SIGNER_ID
SIGNER_ID.comment: The signer is the account that originally signed the transaction that began the blockchain activity, which may or may not include cross-contract calls. If a function calls results in several cross-contract calls, think of the signer as the account that pushed over the first domino in that chain reaction.

RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED.name: RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED
RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED.comment: A boolean indicating whether the receipt was successfully processed, based on the presence of a Failure message.

_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.name: _PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER
_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Block number grouping for ingestion partition

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp for when the block was inserted into the data warehouse by the Chainwalker.

HORIZON_DECODED_ACTIONS_ID.name: HORIZON_DECODED_ACTIONS_ID
HORIZON_DECODED_ACTIONS_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

_INVOCATION_ID.name: _INVOCATION_ID
_INVOCATION_ID.comment: A job ID to identify the run that last modified a record.

metadata.name: TOKEN_LABELS
metadata.comment: This table contains symbol, contract address and decimal information about tokens on NEAR. Have some token information to add? Open a PR on the MetricsDAO GitHub.

TOKEN.name: TOKEN
TOKEN.comment: The long-form name of the token.

SYMBOL.name: SYMBOL
SYMBOL.comment: The shorthand symbol for the token, as used by common price feeds and exchanges.

TOKEN_CONTRACT.name: TOKEN_CONTRACT
TOKEN_CONTRACT.comment: The contract address corresponding to the token, view details of the contract by following this address on the chain explorer.

DECIMALS.name: DECIMALS
DECIMALS.comment: The number of decimals a raw number should be divided by to decimal adjust. For example, bridged tokens from Ethereum typically use 18 decimals so a raw (unadjusted chain number) of 1000000000000000000 represents 1.

TOKEN_LABELS_ID.name: TOKEN_LABELS_ID
TOKEN_LABELS_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

metadata.name: EZ_ACTIONS
metadata.comment:
This table extracts and processes executed Actions from NEAR receipts - storing the argument data under action_data. FunctionCall data is decoded, where possible, and returned to the action_data object under args.
It combines transaction and receipt information to provide a comprehensive view on each of the actions events.

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

TX_SUCCEEDED.name: TX_SUCCEEDED
TX_SUCCEEDED.comment: Indicates whether the transaction failed or succeeded.

TX_GAS_USED.name: TX_GAS_USED
TX_GAS_USED.comment: Units of gas required to execute this transaction. In raw number format, to adjust divide by POW(10,12)

RECEIPT_ID.name: RECEIPT_ID
RECEIPT_ID.comment: Please note this column is being deprecated in favor of simply "receipt_id".

RECEIPT_PREDECESSOR_ID.name: RECEIPT_PREDECESSOR_ID
RECEIPT_PREDECESSOR_ID.comment:
This is the account that was the immediate caller to the smart contract. If this is a simple transaction (no cross-contract calls) from alice.near to banana.near, the smart contract at banana.near considers Alice the predecessor. In this case, Alice would also be the signer.  
  
Source: https://docs.near.org/tutorials/crosswords/beginner/actions#predecessor-signer-and-current-account

RECEIPT_RECEIVER_ID.name: RECEIPT_RECEIVER_ID
RECEIPT_RECEIVER_ID.comment:
The address of the NEAR account that the receipt was executed on.
Note, this differs from `tx_receiver` and is associated with the receipt within a transaction. It is often a contract on which a method is executed by the `signer_id`.

RECEIPT_SIGNER_ID.name: RECEIPT_SIGNER_ID
RECEIPT_SIGNER_ID.comment: The signer is the account that originally signed the transaction that began the blockchain activity, which may or may not include cross-contract calls. If a function calls results in several cross-contract calls, think of the signer as the account that pushed over the first domino in that chain reaction.

RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED.name: RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED
RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED.comment: A boolean indicating whether the receipt was successfully processed, based on the presence of a Failure message.

RECEIPT_GAS_BURNT.name: RECEIPT_GAS_BURNT
RECEIPT_GAS_BURNT.comment:
The gas used in the object, whether it be the transaction, action or receipt. In the case of a curated model where this column is included alongside a transaction fee, this is the gas burnt during the individual action or receipt.

In raw number format with 16 decimal places, to adjust divide by POW(10,16) or multiply by 1e-16.

ACTION_INDEX.name: ACTION_INDEX
ACTION_INDEX.comment: The index of the current `action_name` and `action_data` in the order in which it appeared in the transaction.

IS_DELEGATED.name: IS_DELEGATED
IS_DELEGATED.comment: This column is a boolean value that indicates whether the executed action is a delegated action. Note: Actions with action_name = 'Delegate' themselves are marked as FALSE, while the actions within them are marked as TRUE.

ACTION_NAME.name: ACTION_NAME
ACTION_NAME.comment: The name of the action performed.

ACTION_DATA.name: ACTION_DATA
ACTION_DATA.comment: A JSON object containing the argument data that was called by the `action_event`, if any.

ACTION_GAS_PRICE.name: ACTION_GAS_PRICE
ACTION_GAS_PRICE.comment: Gas price parsed from the receipt_actions JSON object, specifically from the nested path receipt:Action:gas_price. Used to determine the cost per unit of gas for the specific action.

_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.name: _PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER
_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Block number grouping for ingestion partition

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp for when the block was inserted into the data warehouse by the Chainwalker.

ACTIONS_ID.name: ACTIONS_ID
ACTIONS_ID.comment: The `action_id` is a concatenation of `receipt_id `and the index of the action within a receipt. This is unique for each record. The primary hash depends on the source of the action, as both a transaction and a receipt can process actions.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

_INVOCATION_ID.name: _INVOCATION_ID
_INVOCATION_ID.comment: A job ID to identify the run that last modified a record.
