metadata.name: ATLAS_NFT_TRANSACTIONS
metadata.comment: This incremental dbt model unifies NFT minting and transfer data into a single view, providing a comprehensive look at NFT activities. It captures daily activities by transaction hash, method name, receiver ID, signer ID, owner, and token ID.

ATLAS_NFT_TRANSACTIONS_ID.name: ATLAS_NFT_TRANSACTIONS_ID
ATLAS_NFT_TRANSACTIONS_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

METHOD_NAME.name: METHOD_NAME
METHOD_NAME.comment: Name of the method(s) approved for use. NULL if permission is FullAccess.

RECEIVER_ID.name: RECEIVER_ID
RECEIVER_ID.comment:
The address of the NEAR account that the receipt was executed on.
Note, this differs from `tx_receiver` and is associated with the receipt within a transaction. It is often a contract on which a method is executed by the `signer_id`.

SIGNER_ID.name: SIGNER_ID
SIGNER_ID.comment: The signer is the account that originally signed the transaction that began the blockchain activity, which may or may not include cross-contract calls. If a function calls results in several cross-contract calls, think of the signer as the account that pushed over the first domino in that chain reaction.

OWNER.name: OWNER
OWNER.comment: The owner of the token, which is the signer_id for minting events and the receiver_id for transfers.

TOKEN_ID.name: TOKEN_ID
TOKEN_ID.comment: The identifier of the token being transferred or minted.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp for when the block was inserted into the data warehouse by the Chainwalker.

_INVOCATION_ID.comment: A job ID to identify the run that last modified a record.

_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.name: _PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER
_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Block number grouping for ingestion partition

_MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: _MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
_MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment:
The timestamp at which the underlying record was last modified by an internal process.
This is used for incrementally loading based on when the source data was last modified.

metadata.comment: Parses the blocks JSON files for NEAR.

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

PREV_HASH.name: PREV_HASH
PREV_HASH.comment: The prev_hash taken from block headers.

BLOCK_AUTHOR.name: BLOCK_AUTHOR

GAS_PRICE.name: GAS_PRICE

TOTAL_SUPPLY.name: TOTAL_SUPPLY
TOTAL_SUPPLY.comment: The total_supply taken from block headers.

VALIDATOR_PROPOSALS.name: VALIDATOR_PROPOSALS

metadata.name: BRIDGE_RAINBOW
metadata.comment: Extracts data from actions table to build a view of bridge activity through the Rainbow Bridge. Methods defined and explained [here](https://github.com/aurora-is-near/rainbow-token-connector/tree)

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

TOKEN_ADDRESS.name: TOKEN_ADDRESS
TOKEN_ADDRESS.comment: The contract address corresponding to the token, view details of the contract by following this address on the chain explorer.

AMOUNT_RAW.name: AMOUNT_RAW
AMOUNT_RAW.comment: An unadjusted amount (of tokens, price, etc.) for the relevant record. This is the number as it appears on chain and is not decimal adjusted.

MEMO.name: MEMO
MEMO.comment: Memo: The log message within Near now includes an optional dictionary.

DESTINATION_ADDRESS.name: DESTINATION_ADDRESS
DESTINATION_ADDRESS.comment: The address tokens are being sent to, in a transfer or bridge transaction.

SOURCE_ADDRESS.name: SOURCE_ADDRESS
SOURCE_ADDRESS.comment:
The originating address from which tokens are being sent, in a transfer or bridge transaction.  

This addressed is ascertained as best as possible, but may be null depending on the available information.  

Example 1 - tx CzPRdoiU74bo9yqqt9fCP5Xdab2mPVqxr9gU5xeaQA4a stores just "signer_public_key 0x0x1589…f694" on chain.  

Example 2 - tx FA7tQeGQnhminbas28YgaizwmT4vnEUiCr3vdXmN3Xjn does not log any signing address at all.

DESTINATION_CHAIN_ID.name: DESTINATION_CHAIN_ID
DESTINATION_CHAIN_ID.comment:
Chain ID as defined by the corresponding bridge.

Wormhole Portal Bridge https://docs.wormhole.com/wormhole/reference/constants

Multichain Bridge https://docs.multichain.org/getting-started/introduction/supported-chains

Allbridge Classic https://github.com/allbridge-io/allbridge-contract-docs?tab=readme-ov-file#blockchain-ids

SOURCE_CHAIN_ID.name: SOURCE_CHAIN_ID
SOURCE_CHAIN_ID.comment:
Chain ID as defined by the corresponding bridge.

Wormhole Portal Bridge https://docs.wormhole.com/wormhole/reference/constants

Multichain Bridge https://docs.multichain.org/getting-started/introduction/supported-chains

Allbridge Classic https://github.com/allbridge-io/allbridge-contract-docs?tab=readme-ov-file#blockchain-ids

RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED.name: RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED
RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED.comment: A boolean indicating whether the receipt was successfully processed, based on the presence of a Failure message.

METHOD_NAME.name: METHOD_NAME
METHOD_NAME.comment: Name of the method(s) approved for use. NULL if permission is FullAccess.

BRIDGE_ADDRESS.name: BRIDGE_ADDRESS
BRIDGE_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the deployed contract for the token, nft, or other smart contract.

DIRECTION.name: DIRECTION
DIRECTION.comment:
Direction of funds through a bridge. Inbound = tokens bridged to Near ecosystem.

Note - for the Rainbow bridge, inbound means Near or Aurora as tokens pass through Near to get to Aurora.

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp for when the block was inserted into the data warehouse by the Chainwalker.

_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Block number grouping for ingestion partition

AMOUNT_ADJ.comment: A decimal adjusted amount (of tokens, price, etc.).

PLATFORM.name: PLATFORM
PLATFORM.comment: The protocol utilized for the operation, likely a swap or bridge.

BRIDGE_RAINBOW_ID.name: BRIDGE_RAINBOW_ID
BRIDGE_RAINBOW_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

_INVOCATION_ID.comment: A job ID to identify the run that last modified a record.

metadata.name: STREAMLINE_TRANSACTIONS_FINAL
metadata.comment: Singular transaction objects with the shard id and chunk hash from which it was included.

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

SIGNATURE.name: SIGNATURE
SIGNATURE.comment: The signature taken from block headers.

GAS_USED.name: GAS_USED
GAS_USED.comment: Units of gas required to execute this transaction. In raw number format, to adjust divide by POW(10,12)

TRANSACTION_FEE.name: TRANSACTION_FEE
TRANSACTION_FEE.comment:
Total fee paid in NEAR to execute this transaction, which is a summation of all fees paid for actions and receipts within the transaction.

In raw number format, to adjust divide by POW(10,24)

_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.name: _PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER
_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Block number grouping for ingestion partition

ATTACHED_GAS.name: ATTACHED_GAS
ATTACHED_GAS.comment: Units of gas (figure is not decimal adjusted, 10^12) attached to the transaction (this is often higher than 'Gas Used').

TX_SUCCEEDED.name: TX_SUCCEEDED
TX_SUCCEEDED.comment: Indicates whether the transaction failed or succeeded.

TX_STATUS.name: TX_STATUS
TX_STATUS.comment: /// DEPRECATION WARNING /// Indicates whether the transaction failed or succeeded. This will be get deprecated in the future. Please use `tx_succeeded` instead.

metadata.name: EZ_LENDING
metadata.comment: Modeled activity for lending, with token contract and price info joined in for ease of use.

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

SENDER_ID.name: SENDER_ID
SENDER_ID.comment: The id, or wallet address, of the sender from the receipt action.

ACTIONS.name: ACTIONS
ACTIONS.comment:
The action that the user is taking.
Deposit: user is depositing funds to be used for lending
Withdraw: user has changed their mind and are no longer willing to lend, so they withdraw their asset

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the deployed contract for the token, nft, or other smart contract.

TOKEN_ADDRESS.name: TOKEN_ADDRESS
TOKEN_ADDRESS.comment: The contract address corresponding to the token, view details of the contract by following this address on the chain explorer.

SYMBOL.name: SYMBOL
SYMBOL.comment: The shorthand symbol for the token, as used by common price feeds and exchanges.

AMOUNT_RAW.name: AMOUNT_RAW
AMOUNT_RAW.comment: An unadjusted amount (of tokens, price, etc.) for the relevant record. This is the number as it appears on chain and is not decimal adjusted.

AMOUNT_ADJ.name: AMOUNT_ADJ
AMOUNT_ADJ.comment: A decimal adjusted amount (of tokens, price, etc.).

AMOUNT.name: AMOUNT
AMOUNT.comment:
The amount of tokens transferred
If in the ez_ view, this number is decimal adjusted to actual amount. If in silver level or a fact_ view, the raw amount is presented

AMOUNT_USD.name: AMOUNT_USD
AMOUNT_USD.comment: Token amount for the record decimal adjusted and multiplied by a price in USD, if available.

EZ_LENDING_ID.name: EZ_LENDING_ID
EZ_LENDING_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

metadata.name: POOL_EVENTS
metadata.comment: Flattened logs from receipt events where the recipient is a staking pool, for use in multiple downstream models.

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID.name: RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID
RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID.comment: Please note this column is being deprecated in favor of simply "receipt_id".

RECEIVER_ID.name: RECEIVER_ID
RECEIVER_ID.comment:
The address of the NEAR account that the receipt was executed on.
Note, this differs from `tx_receiver` and is associated with the receipt within a transaction. It is often a contract on which a method is executed by the `signer_id`.

SIGNER_ID.name: SIGNER_ID
SIGNER_ID.comment: The signer is the account that originally signed the transaction that began the blockchain activity, which may or may not include cross-contract calls. If a function calls results in several cross-contract calls, think of the signer as the account that pushed over the first domino in that chain reaction.

PREDECESSOR_ID.name: PREDECESSOR_ID
PREDECESSOR_ID.comment:
This is the account that was the immediate caller to the smart contract. If this is a simple transaction (no cross-contract calls) from alice.near to banana.near, the smart contract at banana.near considers Alice the predecessor. In this case, Alice would also be the signer.  
  
Source: https://docs.near.org/tutorials/crosswords/beginner/actions#predecessor-signer-and-current-account

LOG.name: LOG
LOG.comment: A single log extracted from a receipt.

metadata.name: LOGS_S3
metadata.comment: This table extracts all logs from receipts and decodes the arguments for easy use.

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID.name: RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID
RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID.comment: Please note this column is being deprecated in favor of simply "receipt_id".

LOG_ID.name: LOG_ID
LOG_ID.comment: Concatenation between receipt id and index of the log.

LOG_INDEX.name: LOG_INDEX
LOG_INDEX.comment: The positon index for the record in question, if it was stored in a particular order.

RECEIVER_ID.name: RECEIVER_ID
RECEIVER_ID.comment:
The address of the NEAR account that the receipt was executed on.
Note, this differs from `tx_receiver` and is associated with the receipt within a transaction. It is often a contract on which a method is executed by the `signer_id`.

PREDECESSOR_ID.name: PREDECESSOR_ID
PREDECESSOR_ID.comment:
This is the account that was the immediate caller to the smart contract. If this is a simple transaction (no cross-contract calls) from alice.near to banana.near, the smart contract at banana.near considers Alice the predecessor. In this case, Alice would also be the signer.  
  
Source: https://docs.near.org/tutorials/crosswords/beginner/actions#predecessor-signer-and-current-account

SIGNER_ID.name: SIGNER_ID
SIGNER_ID.comment: The signer is the account that originally signed the transaction that began the blockchain activity, which may or may not include cross-contract calls. If a function calls results in several cross-contract calls, think of the signer as the account that pushed over the first domino in that chain reaction.

CLEAN_LOG.name: CLEAN_LOG
CLEAN_LOG.comment: The logs linked to the transaction.

IS_STANDARD.name: IS_STANDARD
IS_STANDARD.comment: A boolean value indicating whether it is a standardized log or not.

GAS_BURNT.name: GAS_BURNT
GAS_BURNT.comment:
The gas used in the object, whether it be the transaction, action or receipt. In the case of a curated model where this column is included alongside a transaction fee, this is the gas burnt during the individual action or receipt.

In raw number format with 16 decimal places, to adjust divide by POW(10,16) or multiply by 1e-16.

RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED.name: RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED
RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED.comment: A boolean indicating whether the receipt was successfully processed, based on the presence of a Failure message.

_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.name: _PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER
_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Block number grouping for ingestion partition

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp for when the block was inserted into the data warehouse by the Chainwalker.

LOGS_ID.name: LOGS_ID
LOGS_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

_INVOCATION_ID.name: _INVOCATION_ID
_INVOCATION_ID.comment: A job ID to identify the run that last modified a record.

metadata.name: EZ_PRICES_HOURLY
metadata.comment: A convenience table for determining token prices by address and blockchain, and native asset prices by symbol and blockchain. This data set is highly curated and contains metadata for one price per hour per unique asset and blockchain.

HOUR.name: HOUR
HOUR.comment: Hour that the price was recorded at.

TOKEN_ADDRESS.name: TOKEN_ADDRESS
TOKEN_ADDRESS.comment: The specific address representing the asset on a specific platform. This will be NULL if referring to a native asset.

DECIMALS.name: DECIMALS
DECIMALS.comment: The number of decimals for the asset. May be NULL.

PRICE.name: PRICE
PRICE.comment: Closing price of the recorded hour in USD.

BLOCKCHAIN.name: BLOCKCHAIN
BLOCKCHAIN.comment: The Blockchain, Network, or Platform for this asset.

IS_NATIVE.name: IS_NATIVE
IS_NATIVE.comment: A flag indicating assets native to the respective blockchain.

IS_DEPRECATED.name: IS_DEPRECATED
IS_DEPRECATED.comment: A flag indicating if the asset is deprecated or no longer supported by the provider.

IS_IMPUTED.name: IS_IMPUTED
IS_IMPUTED.comment: A flag indicating if the price was imputed, or derived, from the last arriving record. This is generally used for tokens with low-liquidity or inconsistent reporting.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

EZ_PRICES_HOURLY_ID.name: EZ_PRICES_HOURLY_ID
EZ_PRICES_HOURLY_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

metadata.name: ATLAS_SUPPLY_DAILY_LOCKUP_LOCKED_BALANCES
metadata.comment: Calculates daily balance of locked balance on a per lockup account-basis.

LOCKUP_ACCOUNT_ID.name: LOCKUP_ACCOUNT_ID
LOCKUP_ACCOUNT_ID.comment: A vault created by the lockup action that holds the distributed NEAR tokens until the end of the lockup duration.

LOCKUP_INDEX.name: LOCKUP_INDEX
LOCKUP_INDEX.comment: An ordinal representation of the lockup account, in block_timestamp order.

OWNER_ACCOUNT_ID.name: OWNER_ACCOUNT_ID
OWNER_ACCOUNT_ID.comment: The wallet address of the owner for the locked tokens, per the initial distribution.

UTC_DATE.name: UTC_DATE
UTC_DATE.comment: Datestamp, assuming UTC-0, of the record.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

LOCKUP_AMOUNT.name: LOCKUP_AMOUNT
LOCKUP_AMOUNT.comment: The amount, in NEAR, of the initial lockup.

DEPOSIT_TIMESTAMP.name: DEPOSIT_TIMESTAMP
DEPOSIT_TIMESTAMP.comment: Block timestamp of the initial deposit.

LOCKUP_TIMESTAMP.name: LOCKUP_TIMESTAMP
LOCKUP_TIMESTAMP.comment: The initial lockup timestamp, in epoch time.

LOCKUP_END_TIMESTAMP.name: LOCKUP_END_TIMESTAMP
LOCKUP_END_TIMESTAMP.comment: When the lockup period ends, per the lockup config on chain.

LOCKUP_TIME_LEFT_NS.name: LOCKUP_TIME_LEFT_NS
LOCKUP_TIME_LEFT_NS.comment: Time left in the lockup, for the date of record at 00:00 UTC.

UNRELEASED_AMOUNT.name: UNRELEASED_AMOUNT
UNRELEASED_AMOUNT.comment: Amount of NEAR yet to be unlocked, as of the date of record.

VESTING_START_TIMESTAMP.name: VESTING_START_TIMESTAMP
VESTING_START_TIMESTAMP.comment: The start of the vesting period.

VESTING_CLIFF_TIMESTAMP.name: VESTING_CLIFF_TIMESTAMP
VESTING_CLIFF_TIMESTAMP.comment: Timestamp for a cliff-style unlock of NEAR, if applicable to this lockup.

VESTING_END_TIMESTAMP.name: VESTING_END_TIMESTAMP
VESTING_END_TIMESTAMP.comment: End of the vesting period, if applicable.

TERMINATE_VESTING_TIMESTAMP.name: TERMINATE_VESTING_TIMESTAMP
TERMINATE_VESTING_TIMESTAMP.comment: Timestamp of when the vesting is to be terminated, if applicable.

TERMINATION_UNVESTED_AMOUNT.name: TERMINATION_UNVESTED_AMOUNT
TERMINATION_UNVESTED_AMOUNT.comment: Unvested amount upon termination of lockup, if applicable.

VESTING_TIME_LEFT_NS.name: VESTING_TIME_LEFT_NS
VESTING_TIME_LEFT_NS.comment: Time left, in ns, until the lockup is fully vested, if applicable.

VESTING_TOTAL_TIME_NS.name: VESTING_TOTAL_TIME_NS
VESTING_TOTAL_TIME_NS.comment: The total amount of time, in ns, of the vesting period, if applicable.

TERMINATION_WITHDRAWN_AMOUNT.name: TERMINATION_WITHDRAWN_AMOUNT
TERMINATION_WITHDRAWN_AMOUNT.comment: Amount, in NEAR, withdrawn at termination of the vesting period, if applicable.

UNVESTED_AMOUNT.name: UNVESTED_AMOUNT
UNVESTED_AMOUNT.comment: Unvested NEAR at time of record.

UNLOCKED_AMOUNT_TODAY.name: UNLOCKED_AMOUNT_TODAY
UNLOCKED_AMOUNT_TODAY.comment: Amount of NEAR unlocked on the date of record.

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp for when the block was inserted into the data warehouse by the Chainwalker.

_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.name: _PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER
_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Block number grouping for ingestion partition

ATLAS_DAILY_LOCKUP_LOCKED_BALANCES_ID.name: ATLAS_DAILY_LOCKUP_LOCKED_BALANCES_ID
ATLAS_DAILY_LOCKUP_LOCKED_BALANCES_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

_INVOCATION_ID.name: _INVOCATION_ID
_INVOCATION_ID.comment: A job ID to identify the run that last modified a record.

metadata.name: NFT_SERIES_METADATA
metadata.comment: NFT Series-level metadata provided by the Pagoda NFT endpoint.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the deployed contract for the token, nft, or other smart contract.

SERIES_ID.name: SERIES_ID
SERIES_ID.comment: The series, or collection, id for a NFT project. Many platforms issues NFT collections from a marketplace contract. For example, the contract `x.paras.near` is the Paras NFT Marketplace and mints NFTs from this master-contract. A token ID 1234:123 indicates that the NFT is token `123` within series `1234`.

METADATA_ID.name: METADATA_ID
METADATA_ID.comment: A MD5 Hash of an NFT contract and series ID, providing an unique indicator for collecting NFT Series Metadata.

CONTRACT_METADATA.name: CONTRACT_METADATA
CONTRACT_METADATA.comment: Token contract metadata from the Pagoda API. Should correspond with the metadata provided by the Nearblocks API.

TOKEN_METADATA.name: TOKEN_METADATA
TOKEN_METADATA.comment: NFT token-level metadata for a NFT series / collection.

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp for when the block was inserted into the data warehouse by the Chainwalker.

_REQUEST_TIMESTAMP.name: _REQUEST_TIMESTAMP
_REQUEST_TIMESTAMP.comment: Time (in UTC) when an API call was made.

NFT_SERIES_METADATA_ID.name: NFT_SERIES_METADATA_ID
NFT_SERIES_METADATA_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

_INVOCATION_ID.name: _INVOCATION_ID
_INVOCATION_ID.comment: A job ID to identify the run that last modified a record.

metadata.name: RECEIPTS
metadata.comment: Filtered receipts where the signer or receiver is the contract nearsocial.near.

RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID.name: RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID
RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID.comment: Please note this column is being deprecated in favor of simply "receipt_id".

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

RECEIPT_INDEX.name: RECEIPT_INDEX
RECEIPT_INDEX.comment: Deprecation notice! Please note this column will be removed from the receipts tables. For more context on NEAR Receipts, please see the announcement in Discord here: https://discord.com/channels/784442203187314689/992655061517881385/1098015182103523408

RECEIPT_ACTIONS.name: RECEIPT_ACTIONS
RECEIPT_ACTIONS.comment: A single receipt produced as a byproduct of the actions taken within a transaction.

EXECUTION_OUTCOME.name: EXECUTION_OUTCOME
EXECUTION_OUTCOME.comment: The outcome of the transaction, action, or receipt that was executed in the shard.

RECEIPT_OUTCOME_ID.name: RECEIPT_OUTCOME_ID
RECEIPT_OUTCOME_ID.comment: Unique identifier(s) (array) of the remaining receipt outcomes for this transaction. This list will not include the record's own receipt id, but will list the subsequent receipts within the transaction.

RECEIVER_ID.name: RECEIVER_ID
RECEIVER_ID.comment:
The address of the NEAR account that the receipt was executed on.
Note, this differs from `tx_receiver` and is associated with the receipt within a transaction. It is often a contract on which a method is executed by the `signer_id`.

SIGNER_ID.name: SIGNER_ID
SIGNER_ID.comment: The signer is the account that originally signed the transaction that began the blockchain activity, which may or may not include cross-contract calls. If a function calls results in several cross-contract calls, think of the signer as the account that pushed over the first domino in that chain reaction.

RECEIPT_TYPE.name: RECEIPT_TYPE
RECEIPT_TYPE.comment: The type of receipt, as taken from the actions executed during the receipt. Options, presently, are Action or Data.

GAS_BURNT.name: GAS_BURNT
GAS_BURNT.comment:
The gas used in the object, whether it be the transaction, action or receipt. In the case of a curated model where this column is included alongside a transaction fee, this is the gas burnt during the individual action or receipt.

In raw number format with 16 decimal places, to adjust divide by POW(10,16) or multiply by 1e-16.

RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED.name: RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED
RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED.comment: A boolean indicating whether the receipt was successfully processed, based on the presence of a Failure message.

METADATA.name: METADATA
METADATA.comment: Metadata (object) for this transaction. May be null, especially for Near Social Widgets.

_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.name: _PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER
_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Block number grouping for ingestion partition

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp for when the block was inserted into the data warehouse by the Chainwalker.

HORIZON_RECEIPTS_ID.name: HORIZON_RECEIPTS_ID
HORIZON_RECEIPTS_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

_INVOCATION_ID.name: _INVOCATION_ID
_INVOCATION_ID.comment: A job ID to identify the run that last modified a record.

metadata.name: FACT_DEX_SWAPS
metadata.comment: Parses log output data for swap information. It was determined logs must be used over inputs in a FunctionCall as only the output contains actual swap information. See tx AfvgkUxP8taJNBLaZYvFumFrrePpJujb2gjQJz7YbRiM as an example.

RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID.name: RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID
RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID.comment: Please note this column is being deprecated in favor of simply "receipt_id".

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

RECEIVER_ID.name: RECEIVER_ID
RECEIVER_ID.comment:
The address of the NEAR account that the receipt was executed on.
Note, this differs from `tx_receiver` and is associated with the receipt within a transaction. It is often a contract on which a method is executed by the `signer_id`.

SIGNER_ID.name: SIGNER_ID
SIGNER_ID.comment: The signer is the account that originally signed the transaction that began the blockchain activity, which may or may not include cross-contract calls. If a function calls results in several cross-contract calls, think of the signer as the account that pushed over the first domino in that chain reaction.

SWAP_INDEX.name: SWAP_INDEX
SWAP_INDEX.comment: The positon index for the record in question, if it was stored in a particular order.

AMOUNT_OUT_RAW.name: AMOUNT_OUT_RAW
AMOUNT_OUT_RAW.comment: Amount out, in a swap or transfer, in adjusted form.

TOKEN_OUT.name: TOKEN_OUT
TOKEN_OUT.comment: The address of the token being swapped to.

AMOUNT_IN_RAW.name: AMOUNT_IN_RAW
AMOUNT_IN_RAW.comment: Amount in, in a swap or transfer, in adjusted form.

TOKEN_IN.name: TOKEN_IN
TOKEN_IN.comment: The address of the token sent for swap.

SWAP_INPUT_DATA.name: SWAP_INPUT_DATA
SWAP_INPUT_DATA.comment:
JSON of input data for the swap, taken from the FunctionCall JSON.
Note, directional keys such as in and out are determined by the pool.

LOG.name: LOG
LOG.comment: The logs linked to the transaction.

FACT_DEX_SWAPS_ID.name: FACT_DEX_SWAPS_ID
FACT_DEX_SWAPS_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

metadata.name: EZ_DEX_SWAPS
metadata.comment: DEX Swaps on the NEAR blockchain, with label and price data joined in for ease of use (where available). Note - view updated February 2024 to use the same v2 Swaps model as fact_dex_swaps. TOKEN_IN and TOKEN_OUT are now SYMBOL_IN/OUT. POOL ID is found in the SWAP_INPUT_DATA, where available.

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

PLATFORM.name: PLATFORM
PLATFORM.comment: The protocol utilized for the operation, likely a swap or bridge.

POOL_ID.name: POOL_ID
POOL_ID.comment: The unique id for the pool involved in the swap.

TRADER.name: TRADER
TRADER.comment: The address who initiated the swap.

SWAP_INDEX.name: SWAP_INDEX
SWAP_INDEX.comment: The index number of the swap indicating its order of execution in the transaction.

AMOUNT_OUT_RAW.name: AMOUNT_OUT_RAW
AMOUNT_OUT_RAW.comment: An unadjusted amount (of tokens, price, etc.) for the relevant record. This is the number as it appears on chain and is not decimal adjusted.

AMOUNT_OUT.name: AMOUNT_OUT
AMOUNT_OUT.comment: The amount of tokens taken out of or received from the swap.

AMOUNT_OUT_USD.name: AMOUNT_OUT_USD
AMOUNT_OUT_USD.comment: Token amount for the record decimal adjusted and multiplied by a price in USD, if available.

TOKEN_OUT_CONTRACT.name: TOKEN_OUT_CONTRACT
TOKEN_OUT_CONTRACT.comment: The address of the deployed contract for the token, nft, or other smart contract.

SYMBOL_OUT.name: SYMBOL_OUT
SYMBOL_OUT.comment: The shorthand symbol for the token, as used by common price feeds and exchanges.

AMOUNT_IN_RAW.name: AMOUNT_IN_RAW
AMOUNT_IN_RAW.comment: An unadjusted amount (of tokens, price, etc.) for the relevant record. This is the number as it appears on chain and is not decimal adjusted.

AMOUNT_IN.name: AMOUNT_IN
AMOUNT_IN.comment: The amount of tokens put into the swap.

AMOUNT_IN_USD.name: AMOUNT_IN_USD
AMOUNT_IN_USD.comment: Token amount for the record decimal adjusted and multiplied by a price in USD, if available.

TOKEN_IN_CONTRACT.name: TOKEN_IN_CONTRACT
TOKEN_IN_CONTRACT.comment: The address of the deployed contract for the token, nft, or other smart contract.

SYMBOL_IN.name: SYMBOL_IN
SYMBOL_IN.comment: The shorthand symbol for the token, as used by common price feeds and exchanges.

SWAP_INPUT_DATA.name: SWAP_INPUT_DATA
SWAP_INPUT_DATA.comment:
JSON of input data for the swap, taken from the FunctionCall JSON.
Note, directional keys such as in and out are determined by the pool.

LOG.name: LOG
LOG.comment: A single log extracted from a receipt.

EZ_DEX_SWAPS_ID.name: EZ_DEX_SWAPS_ID
EZ_DEX_SWAPS_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

EXECUTION_OUTCOME.comment: The outcome of the transaction, action, or receipt that was executed in the shard.

RECEIPT_OUTCOME_ID.name: RECEIPT_OUTCOME_ID
RECEIPT_OUTCOME_ID.comment: Unique identifier(s) (array) of the remaining receipt outcomes for this transaction. This list will not include the record's own receipt id, but will list the subsequent receipts within the transaction.

RECEIVER_ID.name: RECEIVER_ID
RECEIVER_ID.comment:
The address of the NEAR account that the receipt was executed on.
Note, this differs from `tx_receiver` and is associated with the receipt within a transaction. It is often a contract on which a method is executed by the `signer_id`.

SIGNER_ID.name: SIGNER_ID
SIGNER_ID.comment: The signer is the account that originally signed the transaction that began the blockchain activity, which may or may not include cross-contract calls. If a function calls results in several cross-contract calls, think of the signer as the account that pushed over the first domino in that chain reaction.

RECEIPT_TYPE.name: RECEIPT_TYPE
RECEIPT_TYPE.comment: The type of receipt, as taken from the actions executed during the receipt. Options, presently, are Action or Data.

GAS_BURNT.name: GAS_BURNT
GAS_BURNT.comment:
The gas used in the object, whether it be the transaction, action or receipt. In the case of a curated model where this column is included alongside a transaction fee, this is the gas burnt during the individual action or receipt.

In raw number format with 16 decimal places, to adjust divide by POW(10,16) or multiply by 1e-16.

METADATA.name: METADATA
METADATA.comment: Metadata (object) for this transaction. May be null, especially for Near Social Widgets.

RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED.name: RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED
RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED.comment: A boolean indicating whether the receipt was successfully processed, based on the presence of a Failure message.

ERROR_TYPE_0.name: ERROR_TYPE_0
ERROR_TYPE_0.comment: The top-level error type, which likely pertains to the type of object that failed. For example `ActionError` when a action failed to execute for some reason.

ERROR_TYPE_1.name: ERROR_TYPE_1
ERROR_TYPE_1.comment: The second level of error type when a receipt has failed. This pertains to the scope in which the error occurred, such as `FunctionCallError`.

ERROR_TYPE_2.name: ERROR_TYPE_2
ERROR_TYPE_2.comment: The third level of error type which actually pertains to the type of failure. This is commonly `ExecutionError`.

ERROR_MESSAGE.name: ERROR_MESSAGE
ERROR_MESSAGE.comment: The error message, if any, associated with the failure.

_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.name: _PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER
_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Block number grouping for ingestion partition

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp for when the block was inserted into the data warehouse by the Chainwalker.

STREAMLINE_RECEIPTS_FINAL_ID.name: STREAMLINE_RECEIPTS_FINAL_ID
STREAMLINE_RECEIPTS_FINAL_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

_INVOCATION_ID.name: _INVOCATION_ID
_INVOCATION_ID.comment: A job ID to identify the run that last modified a record.

RECEIPT_ID.name: RECEIPT_ID
RECEIPT_ID.comment: The identifying hash for a receipt.

PREDECESSOR_ID.name: PREDECESSOR_ID
PREDECESSOR_ID.comment:
This is the account that was the immediate caller to the smart contract. If this is a simple transaction (no cross-contract calls) from alice.near to banana.near, the smart contract at banana.near considers Alice the predecessor. In this case, Alice would also be the signer.  
  
Source: https://docs.near.org/tutorials/crosswords/beginner/actions#predecessor-signer-and-current-account

metadata.name: ATLAS_NFT_TABLE
metadata.comment: This view model provides a breakdown of NFT transaction activities by receiver_id. It includes counts of unique tokens, transfers within the last 24 hours and 3 days, all transfers, unique owners, total transactions, and minting events.

RECEIVER_ID.name: RECEIVER_ID
RECEIVER_ID.comment: { { doc('receiver_id')}}

TOKENS.name: TOKENS
TOKENS.comment: The count of unique tokens that have been received by the receiver_id.

TRANSFERS_24H.name: TRANSFERS_24H
TRANSFERS_24H.comment: The count of 'nft_transfer' transactions that occurred in the last 24 hours.

TRANSFERS_3D.name: TRANSFERS_3D
TRANSFERS_3D.comment: The count of 'nft_transfer' transactions that occurred in the last 3 days.

OWNERS.name: OWNERS
OWNERS.comment: The count of distinct owners that have interacted with the receiver's tokens.

TRANSACTIONS.name: TRANSACTIONS
TRANSACTIONS.comment: The `tx_count` taken from block headers. The number of transactions included in the block.

MINTS.name: MINTS
MINTS.comment: The count of transactions where the method_name indicates a minting event rather than a transfer.

metadata.name: FACT_STAKING_ACTIONS
metadata.comment:
An updated version of the staking actions table which looks at all logs, instead of just the first receipt.
There are four actions taken when staking: staking->deposit->unstaking->withdraw.
Note - in this core view the amount is decimal adjusted by 10^24.

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID.name: RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID
RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID.comment: Please note this column is being deprecated in favor of simply "receipt_id".

ADDRESS.name: ADDRESS
ADDRESS.comment: The NEAR address of the staking pool.

PREDECESSOR_ID.name: PREDECESSOR_ID
PREDECESSOR_ID.comment:
This is the account that was the immediate caller to the smart contract. If this is a simple transaction (no cross-contract calls) from alice.near to banana.near, the smart contract at banana.near considers Alice the predecessor. In this case, Alice would also be the signer.  
  
Source: https://docs.near.org/tutorials/crosswords/beginner/actions#predecessor-signer-and-current-account

SIGNER_ID.name: SIGNER_ID
SIGNER_ID.comment: The signer is the account that originally signed the transaction that began the blockchain activity, which may or may not include cross-contract calls. If a function calls results in several cross-contract calls, think of the signer as the account that pushed over the first domino in that chain reaction.

ACTION.name: ACTION
ACTION.comment: The staking action performed in this transaction. Can be `"Stake"` or `"Unstake"` if in the deprecating `dim_staking_actions` table, or `staking`, `unstaking`, `deposited`, `withdrawing` if in the new `fact_staking_actions` table. These method names are taken directly from the log in which they occur.

AMOUNT.name: AMOUNT
AMOUNT.comment:
The amount of tokens transferred
If in the ez_ view, this number is decimal adjusted to actual amount. If in silver level or a fact_ view, the raw amount is presented

FACT_STAKING_ACTIONS_ID.name: FACT_STAKING_ACTIONS_ID
FACT_STAKING_ACTIONS_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

metadata.name: FACT_STAKING_POOL_BALANCES
metadata.comment:
Staking pool balances as extracted from receipt logs when an individual makes a staking action.
To calculate balance at a point in time, isolate a single record for each pool. This table is transactional-based, so balances are updated with every staking event by users.
Note - the amount in balance is decimal adjusted by 10^24.

metadata.name: TOKEN_TRANSFER_NATIVE
metadata.comment: This table records all the Transfer actions of the Near blockchain.

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

ACTION_ID.name: ACTION_ID
ACTION_ID.comment: The `action_id` is a concatenation of `receipt_id `and the index of the action within a receipt. This is unique for each record. The primary hash depends on the source of the action, as both a transaction and a receipt can process actions.

AMOUNT_UNADJ.name: AMOUNT_UNADJ
AMOUNT_UNADJ.comment: An unadjusted amount (of tokens, price, etc.) for the relevant record. This is the number as it appears on chain and is not decimal adjusted.

AMOUNT_ADJ.name: AMOUNT_ADJ
AMOUNT_ADJ.comment: A decimal adjusted amount (of tokens, price, etc.).

PREDECESSOR_ID.name: PREDECESSOR_ID
PREDECESSOR_ID.comment:
This is the account that was the immediate caller to the smart contract. If this is a simple transaction (no cross-contract calls) from alice.near to banana.near, the smart contract at banana.near considers Alice the predecessor. In this case, Alice would also be the signer.  
  
Source: https://docs.near.org/tutorials/crosswords/beginner/actions#predecessor-signer-and-current-account

RECEIVER_ID.name: RECEIVER_ID
RECEIVER_ID.comment:
The address of the NEAR account that the receipt was executed on.
Note, this differs from `tx_receiver` and is associated with the receipt within a transaction. It is often a contract on which a method is executed by the `signer_id`.

SIGNER_ID.name: SIGNER_ID
SIGNER_ID.comment: The signer is the account that originally signed the transaction that began the blockchain activity, which may or may not include cross-contract calls. If a function calls results in several cross-contract calls, think of the signer as the account that pushed over the first domino in that chain reaction.

RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED.name: RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED
RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED.comment: A boolean indicating whether the receipt was successfully processed, based on the presence of a Failure message.

GAS_PRICE.name: GAS_PRICE
GAS_PRICE.comment: The gas_price taken from block headers, number is not decimal adjusted.

GAS_BURNT.name: GAS_BURNT
GAS_BURNT.comment:
The gas used in the object, whether it be the transaction, action or receipt. In the case of a curated model where this column is included alongside a transaction fee, this is the gas burnt during the individual action or receipt.

In raw number format with 16 decimal places, to adjust divide by POW(10,16) or multiply by 1e-16.

TOKENS_BURNT.name: TOKENS_BURNT
TOKENS_BURNT.comment: The amount of tokens burnt in a transaction or receipt execution.

_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Block number grouping for ingestion partition

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp for when the block was inserted into the data warehouse by the Chainwalker.

TOKEN_TRANSFER_NATIVE_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

_INVOCATION_ID.comment: A job ID to identify the run that last modified a record.

metadata.name: FACT_INTENTS
metadata.comment:
Parsed NEP245 logs from executed Intents onchain. 
As the Near Intents protocol is in active development, this table may be subject to modification to maintain data integrity. The version of the DIP4 standard that is emitted with the executed intent logs is included, as a result.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

RECEIPT_ID.name: RECEIPT_ID
RECEIPT_ID.comment: Please note this column is being deprecated in favor of simply "receipt_id".

RECEIVER_ID.name: RECEIVER_ID
RECEIVER_ID.comment:
The address of the NEAR account that the receipt was executed on.
Note, this differs from `tx_receiver` and is associated with the receipt within a transaction. It is often a contract on which a method is executed by the `signer_id`.

PREDECESSOR_ID.name: PREDECESSOR_ID
PREDECESSOR_ID.comment:
This is the account that was the immediate caller to the smart contract. If this is a simple transaction (no cross-contract calls) from alice.near to banana.near, the smart contract at banana.near considers Alice the predecessor. In this case, Alice would also be the signer.  
  
Source: https://docs.near.org/tutorials/crosswords/beginner/actions#predecessor-signer-and-current-account

LOG_EVENT.name: LOG_EVENT
LOG_EVENT.comment: Type of NEP245 event (e.g., mt_transfer, mt_burn, mt_mint)

LOG_INDEX.name: LOG_INDEX
LOG_INDEX.comment: Sequential index of the log entry within the receipt

LOG_EVENT_INDEX.name: LOG_EVENT_INDEX
LOG_EVENT_INDEX.comment: Position of the event in the log_data array

OLD_OWNER_ID.name: OLD_OWNER_ID
OLD_OWNER_ID.comment: Sender account ID for transfer events

NEW_OWNER_ID.name: NEW_OWNER_ID
NEW_OWNER_ID.comment: Receiver account ID for transfer events

MEMO.name: MEMO
MEMO.comment: Memo: The log message within Near now includes an optional dictionary.

AMOUNT_INDEX.name: AMOUNT_INDEX
AMOUNT_INDEX.comment: Position of the amount in the flattened amounts array from the NEP245 event

AMOUNT_RAW.name: AMOUNT_RAW
AMOUNT_RAW.comment: An unadjusted amount (of tokens, price, etc.) for the relevant record. This is the number as it appears on chain and is not decimal adjusted.

TOKEN_ID.name: TOKEN_ID
TOKEN_ID.comment: The identifier of the token being transferred or minted.

REFERRAL.name: REFERRAL
REFERRAL.comment: ID of the referring protocol, as extracted from the Receipt log.

DIP4_VERSION.name: DIP4_VERSION
DIP4_VERSION.comment: Version of the DIP4 standard that emitted the log for this action.

GAS_BURNT.name: GAS_BURNT

DESTINATION_ADDRESS.comment: The address tokens are being sent to, in a transfer or bridge transaction.

SOURCE_ADDRESS.name: SOURCE_ADDRESS
SOURCE_ADDRESS.comment:
The originating address from which tokens are being sent, in a transfer or bridge transaction.  

This addressed is ascertained as best as possible, but may be null depending on the available information.  

Example 1 - tx CzPRdoiU74bo9yqqt9fCP5Xdab2mPVqxr9gU5xeaQA4a stores just "signer_public_key 0x0x1589…f694" on chain.  

Example 2 - tx FA7tQeGQnhminbas28YgaizwmT4vnEUiCr3vdXmN3Xjn does not log any signing address at all.

PLATFORM.name: PLATFORM
PLATFORM.comment: The protocol utilized for the operation, likely a swap or bridge.

BRIDGE_ADDRESS.name: BRIDGE_ADDRESS
BRIDGE_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the deployed contract for the token, nft, or other smart contract.

DESTINATION_CHAIN.name: DESTINATION_CHAIN
DESTINATION_CHAIN.comment: The recipient blockchain in a bridge transaction.

SOURCE_CHAIN.name: SOURCE_CHAIN
SOURCE_CHAIN.comment: The originating blockchain in a bridge transaction.

METHOD_NAME.name: METHOD_NAME
METHOD_NAME.comment: Name of the method(s) approved for use. NULL if permission is FullAccess.

DIRECTION.name: DIRECTION
DIRECTION.comment:
Direction of funds through a bridge. Inbound = tokens bridged to Near ecosystem.

Note - for the Rainbow bridge, inbound means Near or Aurora as tokens pass through Near to get to Aurora.

RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED.name: RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED
RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED.comment: A boolean indicating whether the receipt was successfully processed, based on the presence of a Failure message.

FACT_BRIDGE_ACTIVITY_ID.name: FACT_BRIDGE_ACTIVITY_ID
FACT_BRIDGE_ACTIVITY_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

metadata.name: FACT_TRANSACTIONS
metadata.comment: Transactions on the NEAR blockchain.

TRANSACTION_FEE.name: TRANSACTION_FEE

TX_SUCCEEDED.comment: Indicates whether the transaction failed or succeeded.

metadata.name: FACT_ACTIONS_EVENTS_FUNCTION_CALL
metadata.comment:
Deprecating soon! Please migrate to using the new `core__ez_actions` table. All FunctionCalls are decoded in the args element where action_name = 'FunctionCall'.
This view will be removed in Q1 2025.

ACTION_ID.name: ACTION_ID
ACTION_ID.comment: The `action_id` is a concatenation of `receipt_id `and the index of the action within a receipt. This is unique for each record. The primary hash depends on the source of the action, as both a transaction and a receipt can process actions.

RECEIVER_ID.name: RECEIVER_ID
RECEIVER_ID.comment:
The address of the NEAR account that the receipt was executed on.
Note, this differs from `tx_receiver` and is associated with the receipt within a transaction. It is often a contract on which a method is executed by the `signer_id`.

PREDECESSOR_ID.name: PREDECESSOR_ID
PREDECESSOR_ID.comment:
This is the account that was the immediate caller to the smart contract. If this is a simple transaction (no cross-contract calls) from alice.near to banana.near, the smart contract at banana.near considers Alice the predecessor. In this case, Alice would also be the signer.  
  
Source: https://docs.near.org/tutorials/crosswords/beginner/actions#predecessor-signer-and-current-account

SIGNER_ID.name: SIGNER_ID
SIGNER_ID.comment: The signer is the account that originally signed the transaction that began the blockchain activity, which may or may not include cross-contract calls. If a function calls results in several cross-contract calls, think of the signer as the account that pushed over the first domino in that chain reaction.

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

ACTION_NAME.name: ACTION_NAME
ACTION_NAME.comment: The name of the action performed.

METHOD_NAME.name: METHOD_NAME
METHOD_NAME.comment: Name of the method(s) approved for use. NULL if permission is FullAccess.

ARGS.name: ARGS
ARGS.comment: Decoded (where possible) arguments passed alongside the `method_name`.  If decoding failed, the table will contain the raw message.

DEPOSIT.name: DEPOSIT
DEPOSIT.comment:
Sum of all NEAR tokens transferred from the signing account to the receiver account during receipt processing. This includes tokens sent in a Transfer action(s), and tokens sent in a FunctionCall action(s) with a deposit attached.

In raw number format with 24 decimal places, to adjust divide by POW(10,24) or multiply by 1e-24.

ATTACHED_GAS.name: ATTACHED_GAS
ATTACHED_GAS.comment: Units of gas (figure is not decimal adjusted, 10^12) attached to the transaction (this is often higher than 'Gas Used').

RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED.name: RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED
RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED.comment: A boolean indicating whether the receipt was successfully processed, based on the presence of a Failure message.

FACT_ACTIONS_EVENTS_FUNCTION_CALL_ID.name: FACT_ACTIONS_EVENTS_FUNCTION_CALL_ID
FACT_ACTIONS_EVENTS_FUNCTION_CALL_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

metadata.name: FACT_NFT_MINTS
metadata.comment: This table records all the NFT Mints per the NEP171 standard, across various methods.

RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID.name: RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID
RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID.comment: Please note this column is being deprecated in favor of simply "receipt_id".

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

TOKEN_ID.name: TOKEN_ID
TOKEN_ID.comment: The token ID for this NFT contract.

METHOD_NAME.name: METHOD_NAME
METHOD_NAME.comment: Name of the method(s) approved for use. NULL if permission is FullAccess.

ARGS.name: ARGS
ARGS.comment: Decoded (where possible) arguments passed alongside the `method_name`.  If decoding failed, the table will contain the raw message.

MEMO.name: MEMO
MEMO.comment: Memo: The log message within Near now includes an optional dictionary.

DEPOSIT.name: DEPOSIT
DEPOSIT.comment:
Sum of all NEAR tokens transferred from the signing account to the receiver account during receipt processing. This includes tokens sent in a Transfer action(s), and tokens sent in a FunctionCall action(s) with a deposit attached.

In raw number format with 24 decimal places, to adjust divide by POW(10,24) or multiply by 1e-24.

RECEIVER_ID.name: RECEIVER_ID
RECEIVER_ID.comment:
The address of the NEAR account that the receipt was executed on.
Note, this differs from `tx_receiver` and is associated with the receipt within a transaction. It is often a contract on which a method is executed by the `signer_id`.

SIGNER_ID.name: SIGNER_ID
SIGNER_ID.comment: The signer is the account that originally signed the transaction that began the blockchain activity, which may or may not include cross-contract calls. If a function calls results in several cross-contract calls, think of the signer as the account that pushed over the first domino in that chain reaction.

OWNER_PER_TX.name: OWNER_PER_TX
OWNER_PER_TX.comment: Number of different owners batch minting, `data` array's len in the LOG message

MINT_PER_TX.name: MINT_PER_TX
MINT_PER_TX.comment: Number of NFTs minted per `nft_mint` event called.

GAS_BURNT.name: GAS_BURNT
GAS_BURNT.comment:
The gas used in the object, whether it be the transaction, action or receipt. In the case of a curated model where this column is included alongside a transaction fee, this is the gas burnt during the individual action or receipt.

In raw number format with 16 decimal places, to adjust divide by POW(10,16) or multiply by 1e-16.

TRANSACTION_FEE.name: TRANSACTION_FEE
TRANSACTION_FEE.comment:
Total fee paid in NEAR to execute this transaction, which is a summation of all fees paid for actions and receipts within the transaction.

In raw number format, to adjust divide by POW(10,24)

TX_STATUS.name: TX_STATUS
TX_STATUS.comment: /// DEPRECATION WARNING /// Indicates whether the transaction failed or succeeded. This will be get deprecated in the future. Please use `tx_succeeded` instead.

FACT_NFT_MINTS_ID.name: FACT_NFT_MINTS_ID
FACT_NFT_MINTS_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: POOL_BALANCES
metadata.comment:
Staking pool balances as extracted from receipt logs when an individual makes a staking action.
To calculate balance at a point in time, isolate a single record for each pool. This table is transactional-based, so balances are updated with every staking event by users.

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID.name: RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID
RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID.comment: Please note this column is being deprecated in favor of simply "receipt_id".

RECEIVER_ID.name: RECEIVER_ID
RECEIVER_ID.comment:
The address of the NEAR account that the receipt was executed on.
Note, this differs from `tx_receiver` and is associated with the receipt within a transaction. It is often a contract on which a method is executed by the `signer_id`.

SIGNER_ID.name: SIGNER_ID
SIGNER_ID.comment: The signer is the account that originally signed the transaction that began the blockchain activity, which may or may not include cross-contract calls. If a function calls results in several cross-contract calls, think of the signer as the account that pushed over the first domino in that chain reaction.

LOG.name: LOG
LOG.comment: A single log extracted from a receipt.

AMOUNT_RAW.name: AMOUNT_RAW
AMOUNT_RAW.comment: An unadjusted amount (of tokens, price, etc.) for the relevant record. This is the number as it appears on chain and is not decimal adjusted.

AMOUNT_ADJ.name: AMOUNT_ADJ
AMOUNT_ADJ.comment: A decimal adjusted amount (of tokens, price, etc.).

_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.name: _PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER
_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Block number grouping for ingestion partition

POOL_BALANCES_ID.name: POOL_BALANCES_ID
POOL_BALANCES_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID.comment: Please note this column is being deprecated in favor of simply "receipt_id".

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

RECEIPT_INDEX.name: RECEIPT_INDEX
RECEIPT_INDEX.comment: Deprecation notice! Please note this column will be removed from the receipts tables. For more context on NEAR Receipts, please see the announcement in Discord here: https://discord.com/channels/784442203187314689/992655061517881385/1098015182103523408

RECEIPT_ACTIONS.name: RECEIPT_ACTIONS
RECEIPT_ACTIONS.comment: A single receipt produced as a byproduct of the actions taken within a transaction.

EXECUTION_OUTCOME.name: EXECUTION_OUTCOME
EXECUTION_OUTCOME.comment: The outcome of the transaction, action, or receipt that was executed in the shard.

RECEIPT_OUTCOME_ID.name: RECEIPT_OUTCOME_ID
RECEIPT_OUTCOME_ID.comment: Unique identifier(s) (array) of the remaining receipt outcomes for this transaction. This list will not include the record's own receipt id, but will list the subsequent receipts within the transaction.

RECEIVER_ID.name: RECEIVER_ID
RECEIVER_ID.comment:
The address of the NEAR account that the receipt was executed on.
Note, this differs from `tx_receiver` and is associated with the receipt within a transaction. It is often a contract on which a method is executed by the `signer_id`.

PREDECESSOR_ID.name: PREDECESSOR_ID
PREDECESSOR_ID.comment:
This is the account that was the immediate caller to the smart contract. If this is a simple transaction (no cross-contract calls) from alice.near to banana.near, the smart contract at banana.near considers Alice the predecessor. In this case, Alice would also be the signer.  
  
Source: https://docs.near.org/tutorials/crosswords/beginner/actions#predecessor-signer-and-current-account

SIGNER_ID.name: SIGNER_ID
SIGNER_ID.comment: The signer is the account that originally signed the transaction that began the blockchain activity, which may or may not include cross-contract calls. If a function calls results in several cross-contract calls, think of the signer as the account that pushed over the first domino in that chain reaction.

RECEIPT_TYPE.name: RECEIPT_TYPE
RECEIPT_TYPE.comment: The type of receipt, as taken from the actions executed during the receipt. Options, presently, are Action or Data.

GAS_BURNT.name: GAS_BURNT
GAS_BURNT.comment:
The gas used in the object, whether it be the transaction, action or receipt. In the case of a curated model where this column is included alongside a transaction fee, this is the gas burnt during the individual action or receipt.

In raw number format with 16 decimal places, to adjust divide by POW(10,16) or multiply by 1e-16.

metadata.name: DEX_SWAPS_V2
metadata.comment: Parses log output data for swap information. It was determined logs must be used over inputs in a FunctionCall as only the output contains actual swap information. See tx AfvgkUxP8taJNBLaZYvFumFrrePpJujb2gjQJz7YbRiM as an example.

RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID.name: RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID
RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID.comment: Please note this column is being deprecated in favor of simply "receipt_id".

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

RECEIVER_ID.name: RECEIVER_ID
RECEIVER_ID.comment:
The address of the NEAR account that the receipt was executed on.
Note, this differs from `tx_receiver` and is associated with the receipt within a transaction. It is often a contract on which a method is executed by the `signer_id`.

SIGNER_ID.name: SIGNER_ID
SIGNER_ID.comment: The signer is the account that originally signed the transaction that began the blockchain activity, which may or may not include cross-contract calls. If a function calls results in several cross-contract calls, think of the signer as the account that pushed over the first domino in that chain reaction.

SWAP_INDEX.name: SWAP_INDEX
SWAP_INDEX.comment: The positon index for the record in question, if it was stored in a particular order.

AMOUNT_OUT_RAW.name: AMOUNT_OUT_RAW
AMOUNT_OUT_RAW.comment: Amount out, in a swap or transfer, in adjusted form.

TOKEN_OUT.name: TOKEN_OUT
TOKEN_OUT.comment: The address of the token being swapped to.

AMOUNT_IN_RAW.name: AMOUNT_IN_RAW
AMOUNT_IN_RAW.comment: Amount in, in a swap or transfer, in adjusted form.

TOKEN_IN.name: TOKEN_IN
TOKEN_IN.comment: The address of the token sent for swap.

SWAP_INPUT_DATA.name: SWAP_INPUT_DATA
SWAP_INPUT_DATA.comment:
JSON of input data for the swap, taken from the FunctionCall JSON.
Note, directional keys such as in and out are determined by the pool.

LOG.name: LOG
LOG.comment: The logs linked to the transaction.

_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Block number grouping for ingestion partition

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp for when the block was inserted into the data warehouse by the Chainwalker.

DEX_SWAPS_V2_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

metadata.name: ATLAS_SUPPLY
metadata.comment: A table represeting calculations for the supply of NEAR across various categories, such as staked and locked.

UTC_DATE.name: UTC_DATE
UTC_DATE.comment: Datestamp, assuming UTC-0, of the record.

TOTAL_SUPPLY.name: TOTAL_SUPPLY
TOTAL_SUPPLY.comment: The total_supply taken from block headers.

TOTAL_NONSTAKED_SUPPLY.name: TOTAL_NONSTAKED_SUPPLY
TOTAL_NONSTAKED_SUPPLY.comment: Total supply less total staked supply of NEAR.

TOTAL_LOCKED_SUPPLY.name: TOTAL_LOCKED_SUPPLY
TOTAL_LOCKED_SUPPLY.comment: Total NEAR locked by lockup.near disbursements.

STAKED_LOCKED_SUPPLY.name: STAKED_LOCKED_SUPPLY
STAKED_LOCKED_SUPPLY.comment: NEAR supply that is locked via a lockup and staked to a node.

NON_STAKED_LOCKED_SUPPLY.name: NON_STAKED_LOCKED_SUPPLY
NON_STAKED_LOCKED_SUPPLY.comment: NEAR supply that is locked via a lockup but not staked.

STAKED_CIRCULATING_SUPPLY.name: STAKED_CIRCULATING_SUPPLY
STAKED_CIRCULATING_SUPPLY.comment: Non-locked (from a lockup.near disbursement) and staked to a node.

NONSTAKED_CIRCULATING_SUPPLY.name: NONSTAKED_CIRCULATING_SUPPLY
NONSTAKED_CIRCULATING_SUPPLY.comment: NEAR supply that is neither locked nor staked.

PERC_LOCKED_SUPPLY.name: PERC_LOCKED_SUPPLY
PERC_LOCKED_SUPPLY.comment:
Percentage of supply locked.  
total_locked_supply / total_supply

PERC_CIRCULATING_SUPPLY.name: PERC_CIRCULATING_SUPPLY
PERC_CIRCULATING_SUPPLY.comment:
Percentage of NEAR circulating.
circulating_supply / total_supply

PERC_STAKED_LOCKED.name: PERC_STAKED_LOCKED
PERC_STAKED_LOCKED.comment:
Percentage of supply both staked and locked, out of all locked supply.  
locked_and_staked_supply / total_locked_supply

PERC_STAKED_CIRCULATING.name: PERC_STAKED_CIRCULATING
PERC_STAKED_CIRCULATING.comment:
Percentage of supply staked and not locked, of circulating supply.
nonlocked_and_staked_supply / circulating_supply

ATLAS_SUPPLY_ID.name: ATLAS_SUPPLY_ID
ATLAS_SUPPLY_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

_INVOCATION_ID.comment: A job ID to identify the run that last modified a record.

metadata.comment: Fungible Token contract metadata provided by the Nearblocks NFT endpoint.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the deployed contract for the token, nft, or other smart contract.

DECIMALS.name: DECIMALS
DECIMALS.comment: The number of decimals a raw number should be divided by to decimal adjust. For example, bridged tokens from Ethereum typically use 18 decimals so a raw (unadjusted chain number) of 1000000000000000000 represents 1.

ICON.name: ICON
ICON.comment: A base64 encoded image for the token or NFT's icon.

SYMBOL.name: SYMBOL
SYMBOL.comment: The shorthand symbol for the token, as used by common price feeds and exchanges.

DATA.name: DATA

metadata.name: LOCKUP_ACTIONS
metadata.comment: This table records all disbursements by the contract lockup.near.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

DEPOSIT.name: DEPOSIT
DEPOSIT.comment:
Sum of all NEAR tokens transferred from the signing account to the receiver account during receipt processing. This includes tokens sent in a Transfer action(s), and tokens sent in a FunctionCall action(s) with a deposit attached.

In raw number format with 24 decimal places, to adjust divide by POW(10,24) or multiply by 1e-24.

LOCKUP_ACCOUNT_ID.name: LOCKUP_ACCOUNT_ID
LOCKUP_ACCOUNT_ID.comment: A vault created by the lockup action that holds the distributed NEAR tokens until the end of the lockup duration.

OWNER_ACCOUNT_ID.name: OWNER_ACCOUNT_ID
OWNER_ACCOUNT_ID.comment: The wallet address of the owner for the locked tokens, per the initial distribution.

LOCKUP_DURATION.name: LOCKUP_DURATION
LOCKUP_DURATION.comment: The duration of the lockup period.

LOCKUP_TIMESTAMP.name: LOCKUP_TIMESTAMP
LOCKUP_TIMESTAMP.comment: The initial lockup timestamp, in epoch time.

LOCKUP_TIMESTAMP_NTZ.name: LOCKUP_TIMESTAMP_NTZ
LOCKUP_TIMESTAMP_NTZ.comment: Timestamp of the start of the lockup period, converted to UTC time.

RELEASE_DURATION.name: RELEASE_DURATION
RELEASE_DURATION.comment: The duration until the locked tokens can be released.

VESTING_SCHEDULE.name: VESTING_SCHEDULE
VESTING_SCHEDULE.comment: Legacy/no longer used. The vesting schedule for the locked tokens.

TRANSFERS_INFORMATION.name: TRANSFERS_INFORMATION
TRANSFERS_INFORMATION.comment: A dictionary of information about the parameters allowing or limiting transfers of the locked NEAR.

_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Block number grouping for ingestion partition

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp for when the block was inserted into the data warehouse by the Chainwalker.

LOCKUP_ACTIONS_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

_INVOCATION_ID.comment: A job ID to identify the run that last modified a record.

metadata.name: NFT_COMPLETE_NFT_SALES
metadata.comment: This table records all the NFT sales actions of the mainy Near marketplaces..

RECEIPT_ID.name: RECEIPT_ID
RECEIPT_ID.comment: The identifying hash for a receipt.

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

GAS_BURNED.name: GAS_BURNED
GAS_BURNED.comment: Units of gas (figure is not decimal adjusted, 10^12) attached to the transaction (this is often higher than 'Gas Used').

SELLER_ADDRESS.name: SELLER_ADDRESS
SELLER_ADDRESS.comment: The address the sends the token.

TOKEN_ID.name: TOKEN_ID
TOKEN_ID.comment: The identifier of the token being transferred or minted.

METHOD_NAME.name: METHOD_NAME
METHOD_NAME.comment: Name of the method(s) approved for use. NULL if permission is FullAccess.

PRICE.name: PRICE
PRICE.comment: The unadjusted price as reported by the source. This should be decimal adjusted to get the real value.

PRICE_USD.name: PRICE_USD
PRICE_USD.comment: The asset price, in US Dollars.

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp for when the block was inserted into the data warehouse by the Chainwalker.

metadata.name: EZ_NATIVE_DAILY_BALANCES
metadata.comment:
This table records daily balances for NEAR Accounts, according to the parameters set by the Near team's balances indexer. More details around the source data can be found by reading about the NEAR Public Lakehouse on their documentation site: https://docs.near.org/tools/indexing.
Please Note - Flipside does not index or curate the data in this table, we simply provide it as-is from the NEAR Public Lakehouse with a daily sync from the public access table located at `bigquery-public-data.crypto_near_mainnet_us.ft_balances_daily`.

ACCOUNT_ID.name: ACCOUNT_ID
ACCOUNT_ID.comment: The NEAR account address for which the balance is recorded.

EPOCH_BLOCK_ID.name: EPOCH_BLOCK_ID
EPOCH_BLOCK_ID.comment: The max block id in for the epoch.

EPOCH_DATE.name: EPOCH_DATE
EPOCH_DATE.comment: The date and time at which the epoch began

LIQUID.name: LIQUID
LIQUID.comment: The liquid balance of the NEAR account at this epoch.

LOCKUP_ACCOUNT_ID.name: LOCKUP_ACCOUNT_ID
LOCKUP_ACCOUNT_ID.comment: The lockup delegator account ID for the NEAR account, if one exists.

LOCKUP_LIQUID.name: LOCKUP_LIQUID
LOCKUP_LIQUID.comment: Liquid balance of the lockup account at this epoch.

LOCKUP_REWARD.name: LOCKUP_REWARD
LOCKUP_REWARD.comment: Lockup reward balance of the lockup account at this epoch.

LOCKUP_STAKED.name: LOCKUP_STAKED
LOCKUP_STAKED.comment: Lockup staked balance of the lockup account at this epoch.

LOCKUP_UNSTAKED_NOT_LIQUID.name: LOCKUP_UNSTAKED_NOT_LIQUID
LOCKUP_UNSTAKED_NOT_LIQUID.comment: Lockup unstaked but not liquid balance of the lockup account at this epoch.

REWARD.name: REWARD
REWARD.comment: The accumulated reward balance of the NEAR account earned from staking at this epoch.

STAKED.name: STAKED
STAKED.comment: The staked balance of the NEAR account at this epoch.

STORAGE_USAGE.name: STORAGE_USAGE
STORAGE_USAGE.comment: Storage usage of the NEAR account at this epoch.

UNSTAKED_NOT_LIQUID.name: UNSTAKED_NOT_LIQUID
UNSTAKED_NOT_LIQUID.comment: Unstaked but not liquid balance of the NEAR account at this epoch.

EZ_NEAR_DAILY_BALANCES_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

metadata.name: FACT_EVENT_LOGS
metadata.comment: This table contains flattened event logs from transactions on this EVM blockchain. Transactions may have multiple events, which are denoted by the event index for a transaction hash. Therefore, this table is unique on the combination of transaction hash and event index. Please see `fact_decoded_event_logs` or `ez_decoded_event_logs` for the decoded event logs.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed. This field will not be unique in this table, as a given transaction can include multiple events.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address interacted with for a given event.

TOPIC_0.name: TOPIC_0
TOPIC_0.comment: The first topic of the event, which is a unique identifier for the event.

TOPIC_1.name: TOPIC_1
TOPIC_1.comment: The second topic of the event, if applicable.

TOPIC_2.name: TOPIC_2
TOPIC_2.comment: The third topic of the event, if applicable.

TOPIC_3.name: TOPIC_3
TOPIC_3.comment: The fourth topic of the event, if applicable.

EVENT_REMOVED.name: EVENT_REMOVED
EVENT_REMOVED.comment: Whether the event has been removed from the transaction.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address at the transaction level.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The to address at the transaction level.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of this transaction.

TX_SUCCEEDED.name: TX_SUCCEEDED
TX_SUCCEEDED.comment: Whether the transaction was successful, returned as a boolean.

FACT_EVENT_LOGS_ID.name: FACT_EVENT_LOGS_ID
FACT_EVENT_LOGS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was last modified.

TX_STATUS.name: TX_STATUS
TX_STATUS.comment: This column is being deprecated for standardization purposes. Please update your queries to use the `tx_succeeded` column instead by March 10, 2025.

_LOG_ID.name: _LOG_ID
_LOG_ID.comment: This column is being removed for standardization purposes. Please remove or update your queries with `CONCAT(tx_hash, '_', event_index)` to reconstruct `_log_id` by March 10, 2025.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: EZ_DECODED_EVENT_LOGS
metadata.comment:
'For information on how to submit a contract for decoding, as well as how ABIs are sourced, please visit [here](https://science.flipsidecrypto.xyz/abi-requestor/). This model contains decoded event logs for contracts that we have an ABI for. Please note, this table does not include all event logs, only those that we have an ABI for. The `decoded_log` column is the easiest place to query decoded data. It is a JSON object, where the keys are the names of the event parameters, and the values are the values of the event parameters. You can select from this column using the following sample format, `decoded_log:from::string` or more generally, `decoded_log:<event_param>::datatype`. See below for a full sample query. The `full_decoded_logs` column contains the same information, as well as additional fields such as the datatype of the decoded data. You may need to laterally flatten this column to query the data.
      
Sample query for USDC Transfer events:
```sql select  tx_hash, block_number, contract_address, decoded_log:from::string as from_address, decoded_log:to::string as to_address, decoded_log:value::integer as value from ethereum.core.fact_decoded_event_logs where contract_address = lower('0xA0b86991c6218b36c1d19D4a2e9Eb0cE3606eB48') and block_number between 16400000 and 16405000 and event_name = 'Transfer' limit 50```'

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed. This field will not be unique in this table, as a given transaction can include multiple events.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address interacted with for a given event.

TOPIC_0.name: TOPIC_0
TOPIC_0.comment: The first topic of the event, which is a unique identifier for the event.

TOPIC_1.name: TOPIC_1
TOPIC_1.comment: The second topic of the event, if applicable.

TOPIC_2.name: TOPIC_2
TOPIC_2.comment: The third topic of the event, if applicable.

TOPIC_3.name: TOPIC_3
TOPIC_3.comment: The fourth topic of the event, if applicable.

EVENT_REMOVED.name: EVENT_REMOVED
EVENT_REMOVED.comment: Whether the event has been removed from the transaction.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address at the transaction level.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The to address at the transaction level.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of the contract call.

EVENT_NAME.name: EVENT_NAME
EVENT_NAME.comment: The name of the event, as defined in the contract ABI.

FULL_DECODED_LOG.name: FULL_DECODED_LOG
FULL_DECODED_LOG.comment: The full decoded log, including the event name, the event parameters, and the data type of the event parameters.

DECODED_LOG.name: DECODED_LOG
DECODED_LOG.comment: The flattened decoded log, where the keys are the names of the event parameters, and the values are the values of the event parameters.

CONTRACT_NAME.name: CONTRACT_NAME
CONTRACT_NAME.comment: The name of the contract, if the contract has a name() function.

EZ_DECODED_EVENT_LOGS_ID.name: EZ_DECODED_EVENT_LOGS_ID
EZ_DECODED_EVENT_LOGS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

TX_STATUS.name: TX_STATUS
TX_STATUS.comment: This column is being deprecated for standardization purposes. Please update your queries by March 10, 2025.

metadata.name: DIM_CONTRACTS
metadata.comment: This table contains contract details for contracts deployed on the Ethereum, Optimism, Polygon, BSC, Arbitrum, Avalanche, Osmosis and Solana blockchains.

ADDRESS.name: ADDRESS
ADDRESS.comment: The unique address of the deployed contract.

SYMBOL.name: SYMBOL
SYMBOL.comment: The symbol used to represent this contract. Please note this is not necessarily unique.

NAME.name: NAME
NAME.comment: The name of the deployed contract. Please note this is not necessarily unique.

DECIMALS.name: DECIMALS

PARENT_CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the contract that the event abi is for.

SIMPLE_EVENT_NAME.comment: The name of the event along with its parameters.

EVENT_SIGNATURE.name: EVENT_SIGNATURE
EVENT_SIGNATURE.comment: The keccak256 signature of the simple event name. This can be used to filter for events in the logs table on topics[0].
